title: Adapting Cyber Awareness for Evolving Computing Environments
objective:
Cyberspace has become an increasingly contested military domain. To mitigate significant incidents with immediate consequences for both military and civil critical infrastructures, strategic cooperation among EU member states and allied countries in cyber defence is needed. The first step towards effective defence is to obtain situational awareness, which is the basis for any successful counteraction. In particular, cyber situational awareness (CSA) aims at providing all stakeholders and personnel of all levels and forces in the chain of command with accurate information about the current threat landscape, according to the individual needs of the users.

Building a CSA, especially in a cross-domain and cross-country fashion, is a complex task, requiring successful integration of research from different fields, including but not limited to data and CTI collection, collaborative and federated learning, attack behaviour classification, visualisation techniques, cryptogaphy for CTI sharing and trust, or deception technologies and digital twins. However, state of the art solutions do not provide a full integration of all necessary technologies and tools to offer a holistic CSA picture.

The main ambition of NEWSROOM is therefore to overcome the current limitations, by studying all relevant CSA aspects, and designing an integrated CSA platform combining data insights, collaborative intrusion detection for attack classification, and information and CTI sharing considering military standards regarding information security and confidentiality. Advanced visualisation concepts will allow for a mission-specific view at all levels of the command hierarchy. Following a co-creation approach involving all relevant stakeholders, NEWSROOM will identify relevant application scenarios for CSA technoligies in cyber defence, which will be validated in cyber range environments to enable realistic conditions for testing CSA solutions and processes, and training military staff.

title: Sustainable Components for Underwater Acoustics using Lead-free materials in Europe
objective:
The SCUALE (Sustainable Components for Underwater Acoustics using Lead-free materials in Europe) project aims to study, develop and produce advanced lead-free materials and components to replace PZT (Lead Zirconate Titanate) existing ceramics with improved performance for military underwater acoustics applications. The overarching goal of SCUALE project is to initiate the establishment of at least one European supply chain of lead-free piezoelectric materials that are suitable for underwater acoustics military applications. SCUALE consortium led by TDMS, consists of 14 partners representing 8 countries. The studies of the SCUALE project are articulated around three main themes: 
- Advanced lead-free materials and components research: carry out research for the development of advanced lead-free materials and components with physical and piezoelectric properties enabling the substitution of PZT in sonar applications. 
- Improving lead-free piezoelectric materials manufacturing processes: increase the manufacturing readiness level of the most mature lead-free piezoelectric materials and bring out one or more European industrial supplier of lead-free piezoelectric materials for sonar transducers. 
- PZT substitution in sonar transducers: increase the technology maturity of lead-free piezoelectric materials for PZT substitution in sonar applications and assess these new materials for sonar applications on representative test transducers under operational constraints. 

Also, SCUALE will undertake the development of disruptive lead-free ceramic processes such as textured ceramics and 3D printing as well as the development of disruptive fiber-optic sensing providing operational performance improvements in terms of bandwidth, system noise and weight. SCUALE project believes that conducting Life Cycle Analysis (LCA) from the research and early design phases of the new lead-free materials will contribute to ensure that materials foreseen in SCUALE are actually sustainable.

title: Single European Sky and InterOPerability
objective: The Single European Sky and InterOPerability (SESIOP) project brings together a strong team composed of European leaders in the air defence and ATM domain as well small and medium enterprises, research institutes and academia that enlarge the team´s know-how and further underline the emphasis on innovative approach and solutions for military domain that have been so far applied mainly in the civilian sector.  With a large European footprint (14 European nations represented in the team), the SESIOP project  aims to increase interoperability of military Air C2 systems against SES rules thanks to the definition of an architectural solution which will ensure that data exchange between civil-military and military-military control centres uses the latest technologies developed, in particular the achievements reached in the SESAR framework (e.g. SWIM technologies, Trajectory Based Operations etc.) ensuring a seamless evolution towards efficient, secured and safe operations, in planning, tasking and execution phases. The SESIOP project will include a deep analysis of the current “as is” situation in terms of interaction between European Air C2 and ATC centres, and of the operational needs. Close interaction and involvement of national stakeholders – primarily national ministries of defence – will be a very important part of the initial activities of the project that will strongly impact the remaining part of project activities when interoperability requirements will be analysed and defined. Interoperability Interface Requirements for Military and Civil Centres and design of an efficient and exhaustive Architectural Interface Model to support it will be the crucial elements of the technical work. To make sure that the designed Model is realistic, partial tests for risk reduction will be conducted at the end of the 2-year project and will allow to assess feasibility of the architectural interoperability model and its selected parameters.

title: Shared daTabase for Optronics image Recognition and Evaluation
objective:
Forces have an increasing need for AI-based image recognition systems to fasten decision-making, enhance situation awareness, responsiveness, and survivability. The development of these systems is hindered by the lack of representative defence images, and associated tools to share them properly.. 
STORE aims at tackling two critical challenges for the European defence community: i) the optimal development and benchmark of AI-based integrated image recognition systems, and ii) the creation a shared European database on annotated defence images. 
STORE will offer short and medium-term keys to detect and recognize new threats and to counter their evolution. Three use cases built around threat types are addressed by STORE: Battle tanks & Infantry Vehicle threats, Drone & drone swarm threats, and Hypersonic threats. 
 STORE will address 3 main technical and scientific challenges to develop these technologies:  
•	Design of state-of-the-art frameworks for data and code sharing and benchmark 
•	Processing of numerous AI/ML models, leaning on these frameworks, 
•	Development of federated learning methods to enable the share of classified data. 
This foster the development of new integrated image processing offering increased situational awareness, safety, resilience and survivability for the European forces operations where Automatic Target Detection and Recognition, tracking and semantic understanding are decisive features.
STORE leverages on a strong and balanced consortium of 21 partners from 9 countries, bringing together a unique combination of expertise from RTOs/research institutes, innovative SMEs, and major industrial companies at the forefront of optronics, database management and AI-research and development. STORE will reinforce and create new cross-border cooperations. The extensive geographic coverage of EU member states and Norway ensures large compliance with states capability requirements and offers various environments for appropriate training data.
keywords: AI-based image recognition; Optronic sensors; data sharing; database management; software frameworks; data acquisition and annotation; AI algorithms; AI benchmark; image processing

title: SWArm and Teaming operation of manned & unmanned underwater vehicle SHOAL
objective:
The SWAT-SHOAL project is focused on researching technologies that improve collaboration between manned and unmanned assets in missions against moving subsurface threats. The aim is to develop the concept of a System of Systems (SoS) that integrates different types of vehicles in a swarm or squad, organized by teams but working as a single unit, with the goal of achieving greater performance and higher efficiency in the execution of a broad spectrum of missions. 

The use of different vehicle types, as well as the possibility of commanding from different platforms or divers allows to face an extended and more complex range of scenarios, improving team efficiency, leveraging the different capabilities in terms of unmanned vehicles, their sensors and overall performance that each resource can bring into the operational objectives. The diversity of platforms that configure the SoS allows broadening the type of missions to be executed, for example surveillance and reconnaissance, mine-countermeasures, collaborative engagement or support amphibious operations. The project specially considers the execution of missions in confined and shallow waters (CSW), analyzing the specific characteristics of this environment. 

Strengthened cooperation between the engineer and the end user will take place during feasibility studies as well as the design phase to integrate all these components into the SoS demonstrator. SWAT-SHOAL representative functionalities will be implemented to be validated in a laboratory through simulation. A subset of these functionalities will be demonstrated in seawater. This demonstrator will validate the SoS concept and architecture that will be developed under SWAT-SHOAL project.

title: Development of intelligent military capabilities for monitoring, medical care and evacuation for contagious, injured and contaminated personnel
objective:
The situation of ground forces has seen paradigm changes, which also affect the provision of medical care. In any future battlefield, ground forces will cover more extensive areas. Combined with highly dynamic location changes and greater reach of military intelligence as well as an increasing threat of CBRN scenarios, the requirements for the medical environment have risen substantially, especially as the time between injury and support measures must be as short as possible. In addition, the medical care unit in the field cannot be as close to the personnel in the field as this was the case in the past. This poses challenges to the transport chain, which needs to be quick, flexible, coordinated, autonomous, and ideally unmanned.
Despite unmanned ground and aerial transport and en-route care chains have been discussed for years, no such solution has been realised yet. iMEDCAP will provide the design and component level proof-of-concept validation of an integrated solution to detect, extract, transport and remotely provide first support to highly injured and possibly contaminated personnel. This is realised in a fully unmanned setup, making transport capacities easier to scale, while significantly saving medical staff resources.
The iMEDCAP solution will comprise the following activities and technology developments to realise this solution: a user-driven scenario definition taking into account possible CBRN threats as well as recent mission experience, the design and development of autonomous detection of injured personnel, automatic definition of an evacuation and rescue strategy, involving UGV and UAV as needed, both unmanned, and an interoperable patient box, equipped with diagnostic and intervention equipment which can provide initial measures during transport under remote control. The final outcome will be validation of the components and a roadmap for further development, laying the foundation for a modern and digital European Rescue system.

KLINIKUM DER TECHNISCHEN UNIVERSITÄT MÜNCHEN (TUM KLINIKUM)

title: Advanced capabilities & Certification for Tactical UAV Systems
objective:
ACTUS is built to be the most ambitious project related to the domain of tactical RPAS at European scale. 
Tactical RPAS category covers a broad range of small and medium unmanned platforms. Though they draw less attention than Long-Endurance Medium Altitude RPAS (MALE), their volume is significantly higher and their operational impact has been quite high in recent conflicts. They are engaged at the heart of the battlefield where their polyvalence and robustness makes them cost-efficient assets. 
Today they are facing new requirements to operate in higher intensity conflicts and to broaden their spectrum of operational missions. New challenges include a maturity step towards higher design level (through certification), major increase of safety of flight (with new generation flight control system), improved resilience against harsh operating conditions (GNSS-denied environment, communication jamming, icing conditions) and weapon integration using safe design standards.
Gathering the experience and expertise of proven European players in the specific TRPAS domain, ACTUS answers to these demanding requirements and is meant to constitute a significant step in the technological evolution of European tactical RPAS systems.
The ACTUS Consortium consists of 24 partners and a number of specialized subcontractors from 11 countries from South, Central and North Europe, including:• Defence primes: IDE (Coordinator),SED, TLS, SAAB, PATRIA & DIEHL; • Aircraft designers/producers: HAI, ALTUS & CYRIC • Aircraft system designers/producers: ULP, UBIQ, EMB, SED & ROB; • Software/hardware developers: GEO, REAL, AES & ISD; • RTOs: TUM, AIT & UoP • Supporting entities: DBC in charge of ethics & SGX  in charge of dissemination.
The ACTUS Consortium members have extensive background, complementarity with regards to the ACTUS scope and already established co-operations among them. The ACTUS Project is supported by 7 Ministries of Defense from 7 MSs.

title: Artificial Intelligence Deployable Agent
objective:
The project Artificial Intelligence Deployable Agent (AIDA) aims to develop and upgrade European defence technology in the field of cyber defence AI. The project addresses two main challenges that defence sector end-users are facing: 1) A growing attack surface due to increasing digitalisation and 2) the use of AI-based solutions for delivering cyber attacks.

As a solution, the project delivers a common European framework for developing and operating cyber defence AI agents across the entire cyber incident cycle. AIDA shall prototype AI agents for all military domains to TRL7.  The focus is on supporting operators and decision-makers within various scenarios (from preparation through response up to lessons-learned) and technologies (manned-unmanned, fixed-mobile). For successful adoption, due attention is given to AI trustworthiness, both from technical and cultural perspectives.

The solution is intended to be used in conjunction with existing or future cyber defence and security toolsets, as a way to enhance the capabilities and avoid unnecessary duplication. Nonetheless, separate AI management modules are planned within AIDA which have the potential to become a pan-European platform for cyber (defence) AI agents.

The project delivers highly needed benefits to the security and autonomy of the EU (incl., for critical infrastructure) and to the advancement of the EU’s industrial base in the strategic area of AI. In addition, the solution shall alleviate human resource availability problems and promote military mobility through securing modern autonomous vehicles and aircrafts.

title: Enhancing cross-border CBRN operational readiness and effectiveness through an overarching System of Systems approach
objective:
The main idea of CBRN SoS (System of Systems) is to design an overarching information system that can integrate different existing national CBRN defence information systems and components in a modular and flexible way. It can be permanently stationed in a specific configuration of core components ready for on demand deployment. Additional elements from different Nations can be added dynamically depending on specific needs of the operation. 
CBRN SoS addresses Detection, Identification and Monitoring (DIM), Knowledge Management (KM), Physical Protection (PP), and Hazard Management (HM). First an EU CBRN solution inventory study for these components will be conducted. These results will be analysed to identify capability improvements in existing information systems and map out synergies between Member States to define potential entry points for the federated CBRN SoS. Subsequently, the detailed system architecture design will be derived, and key representative parts prototyped to validate the system through demonstrations.
The main advantages of CBRN SoS result from the ability for multiple entities from different Member States to collaborate which enhances the overall situational awareness and leads to a faster and more efficient CBRN threat response. 
Using ATP 45 elements as key pillars for the distributed CBRN SoS software architecture, is an attempt to bring a well-established and known system structure into the fragmented landscape of isolated CBRN solutions. In this way different Nations can relate and map own structures to CBRN SoS more easily, which eventually improves the recognizability and identification of end-users with CBRN SoS. Rather than developing a completely new software solution from scratch, CBRN SoS uses the more efficient and more realistic approach to focus on interfaces to existing solutions and modules, as Member States want to continue using their own solutions and providers but still be able to cooperate in a federated way.

title: European Counter Unmanned Aerial Systems
objective:
E-CUAS aims to the design and development of CUAS solutions for countering class I UAS, single and/or multi-UAS, including military grade UAS such as loitering munitions by integrating multiple technologies for the effective protection of critical defence infrastructure, installations and assets in fixed, mobile and deployed scenarios.
The proposed CUAS solutions will integrate a) relevant passive and active sensors technologies to detect, track, classify the threats, including the capability of detection for non-cooperative and autonomous UAS.  b) a suite of soft kill and hard kill effector technologies with an optimal selection and activation of relevant effectors with a broad set of ROE adapted to the surrounding environment and the operational scenario. c) Command and Control (C2) capabilities supporting minimum operator effort for decision making, effective sensor and effector engagement according to ROE. d) networking and communications infrastructure, cyber security and interoperability data models and protocols for standard interfaces and interoperability with relevant foreseen UAS systems (e.g. U-space) and higher air defence C2 or other units.
The E-CUAS project is in continuity and complements the project JEY-CUAS awarded in the frame of the call EDIDP2020. The E-CUAS project will study, harmonize and consolidate concepts of operations, use cases, user needs, and operational requirements; deliver harmonized system requirements and standardized CUAS open architecture and interfaces; deliver CUAS solutions system/sub-system design; deliver CUAS solutions prototypes tested and qualified in relevant defence scenarios agreed with Member States.
Four large E-CUAS demos in defence scenarios are planned in Italy, Spain, France/Belgium, Germany of CUAS for protection of critical infrastructure, forward operating base and mobile assets. A security scenario in a civilian urban context for the protection of large events in public areas is also addressed.
keywords: CUAS protection of Foward Operating Base

title: European Cyber Situational Awareness Platform - Enhanced Cyberspace Operations
objective:
ECYSAP Enhanced Cyberspace Operations (ECYSAP EYE) will conduct a new development spiral for the Cyberspace Situational Awareness and Situational Understanding (CSASU) capability initiated by EDA contracts (CySAP) and a precedent EDIDP phase (ECYSAP). This new spiral will introduce novel functionalities that responds to additional end-user requirements, Concepts of Employment (CONEMP) of additional member states, and further adaption to the continuous and evolving understanding of how operationalize cyberspace in the context of multi-domain operations. ECYSAP EYE aligns with the needs expressed in the European Union Military Vision and Strategy on Cyberspace as a Domain of Operations, being a key piece for EU CSDP Command and Control (C2) under the PESCO EUMILCOM project. Its increased level of ambition focalizes in:
•To orchestrate reasoning and projection on the perceived mission-centric cyber pictures by Mission Engineering (ME) processes, which allows exploring dependencies between cyber assets and mission context while facilitating analysts in developing customized intelligence products to assist military decision-makers. 
•To assist human beings for their more in-depth comprehension of cyberspace regardless lacking of advanced cyber skills and customized to their area of interests, operations and responsibility. ECYSAP EYE explores cyberspace activities in a continuum of conflicts that intermingle military but also civilian actors, and where cyber situations may present physical, virtual or cyber cognitive nature.
•To transition from a purely defensive posture to understand changes in cyberspace as opportunities, assessing related effects not only in terms of mission assurance (which ECYSAP EYE will also make evolve from EDIDP phase) but also opportunities. ECYSAP EYE will cover specific developments for contributing to ISTAR (Intelligence, surveillance, target acquisition, and reconnaissance). 
•To achieve a more sophisticated development of options, differentiating passive measures from proposals that confront enemy decisions in the physical, virtual and cognitive spheres. ECYSAP EYE provides tailored guidance on how to response threatening situations and/or exploit opportunities. 
•Architectural transition to a System of Systems (SoS), which benefits include easing holistic capability understanding, more effective resource sharing/pooling and adaptation to different Force/C2 structures and procedures.

ECYSAP EYE is an EU sovereign initiative motivated by all defence capability boosting directions indicated by the EU Heads of State or Government as a consequence of Russian aggression to Ukraine (Versailles, March 2022). Passively contributes to 2) step up cooperation through joint projects; 3) close shortfalls and meet EU capability objectives; 4) boost innovation, including through civil/military synergies; and 5) strengthen and develop EU defence industry, including SMEs.

title: EUROPEAN INTERACTIVE SENSOR-BASED DYNAMIC DEFENCE NETWORK
objective:
Today’s EU Integrated Air and Missile Defence (IAMD) sensor centric weapons systems need to be improved to increase their resilience and reactivity against the emergence of new threats and the repercussions that they have. These new threats are stealthier, faster, greater in number, and more agile by their manoeuvrability. The combination of those threats used in synchronized strikes often confuse and saturate current IAMD weapon systems.
The NIAG Working group 260 (NATO Industry Advisory Industry Group) recommended that IAMD suppliers should move away from a sensor centric to a network centric approach through the creation of a network of FCCs and sensors referred to as a ‘sensor grid’ which would allow real time sharing of data and services between sensors in a highly dynamic and efficient manner. 
In this way improvements in operational performance are achieved through a combination of fast data rate dynamic sensor tasking, data fusion, and the effective distribution of services in real-time (below a second) over the dynamic decision-making sensor grid. It obtains the benefit of the multiple sensors coverage, resources and data combined  while accelerating the exchange of information up and down the control and command chain.
The overriding ambition of the project is to define the EISNET architecture framework tool (EAFT) that will consist in a set of common concepts, rules and functions that will permit the scalability of heterogenous weapon systems, sensors and FCC with different architectures or configurations cooperating in a network centric solution. To achieve this, FCC and sensor suppliers will have to implement progressively and seamlessly these concepts, rules and functions in their systems to be compliant with the EAFT. Heterogeneity of technologies often considered as a weakness of the European defence market will become a benefit through the combining of these different sensors, weapons and FCC and use them as a unified system while preserving their autonomy and sovereignty in usage and on key technologies. This is a new tactical advantage as the attacking threats are faced with uncertainty while the diversity of systems with real-time dynamic tactics management contributes to an ever better resilience.

title: European Military Integrated Space Situational Awareness and Recognition capabilitY
objective:
EMISSARY proposal gives a comprehensive response to the growing dependency and threats facing space infrastructure, which are vital for global positioning, navigation, intelligence, and secure communications in military operations. 
The EU Council Conclusions on the EU Space Strategy for Security and Defence underscore the importance of Space Domain Awareness (SDA) for national sovereignty and encourage sharing SDA information among Member States. EMISSARY aligns with these conclusions, aiming to support the establishment of a complete and independent European military SSA value-chain. 

EMISSARY’s value proposition focuses on four interconnected beacons: sovereignty in detection, tracking, and characterization of space objects; shared European recognized space picture to sMS based on enhanced SSA data; integration into the international landscape; and establishing a robust European data security policy. It emphasizes the development of a network of advanced sensors and a military SSA Command and Control (C2) software to process and circulate data, ensuring national and European sovereignty while fostering EDTIB collaboration.

The project involves government agencies, industries, SME and midcaps, scientific communities and RTOs, and integrating international initiatives and innovative technologies. It encompasses 51 partners from 13 Member States, 12 of which has signed the Letter of Intent, ensuring continuity and expansion of expertise from previous projects like INTEGRAL and SAURON. EMISSARY aims to achieve a prototyping, demonstration and qualification stage for a large set of the technologies developed, reflecting a commitment to advancing European military SSA capabilities, ensuring security and sustainability in space, and enhancing Europe's strategic autonomy and international competitiveness in the space domain.

title: European Detect & Avoid System, phase 2
objective:
This project addresses the EDF-2023-DA-C4ISR-DAA topic “Detect and Avoid” (DAA), technologies that enables Remotely Piloted Aircraft Systems (RPAS) to integrate safely into non-segregated civil and military airspace, avoiding collisions with other aircraft. The DAA system observes the environment surrounding the Remotely Piloted Aircraft (RPA), detects traffic, assesses the risk of collision, informs and provides suggestions to the Remote Pilot (RP) to remain well clear and, when appropriate generates a new flight path to avoid collision. This way, DAA enables the RPA, or Unmanned Aerial Systems (UAS), to operate along with other manned and unmanned aircraft, allowing a much more flexible use of RPAS/UAS than today, in order to train for, and conduct, security and defence missions in peacetime, crisis and conflict.
The European Detect and Avoid System - Part 2 project (herein abbreviated “EUDAAS2”) will complete the development of the DAA system for RPAS, designed and validated in the EDIDP 2019 Part 1 (herein abbreviated “EUDAAS1”), delivering (via EUROCAE) a full suite of required DAA standard updates supporting the provision of the ETSO baseline (by EASA), for the future system certification and product industrialization phase. 
The project’s Mission, as defined by the EDF-2023-DA-C4ISR-DAA topic scope, is to provide a fully standardised, qualified and certifiable DAA solution for UAS. The DAA solution should aim at fully integrating RPAS in the general airspace (A-G classes) without any limitations regarding operation in the airspace including in TMA.
The EUDAAS project runs over 4 years and is executed by a core the team maintained from EUDAAS1, supported by 5 contributing Member States and additional 6 nations cross-border partners. The team consist with a mix of with Defence and Aeronautics Industry groups, Aerospace Research Institutes, SMEs/MidCaps and Eurcontrol as the intergovernmental ATM organisation of Europe.

title: Future Indirect fiRes European Solution 2
objective:
The latest conflicts underlined the need of cutting-edge rockets and 155mm artillery guided projectiles able to provide extended range, but also accuracy. Even if some capacities are still available in Europe, FIRES 2 project will place Europe as a future key player in the world.
FIRES 2, as a continuity of the EDIDP FIRES project, proposes an unprecedented approach combining two initially different types of ammunition (155mm artillery shell and rockets) into a single project that stimulates complementary and commonalities at sub-system and component levels. 
The project will prepare a roadmap to develop this future ammunition family with longer range and higher accuracy within the timeframe 2030, offering an artillery projectile able to cover 60km at least, offering a 10m class CEP and a rocket able to cover 150 km range minimum and with a 10m class CEP. For this purpose, FIRES 2 will use FIRES’ output to save time on the research phase, push forward feasibility studies and perform a comprehensive design of the most innovative concepts for the following subsystems: propulsion, seeker, airframe, GNC, communications command and control, payload, computer vision, computational unit and power supply. Furthermore, FIRES 2 intends to carry forward with subsystem prototyping and de-risking tests on key technologies identified as paramount to reach extended range and accuracy: propulsion, seeker, GNC, and fuze (payload). 
The critical subsystems de-risked in FIRES 2 will reach qualification in a subsequent “FIRES 3” project (2028-2030). The European supply chain is expected to achieve an EU critical components production capability to setup the industrialization phase for 2032. This will reinforce EU’s independence and competitiveness addressing concurrent markets by creating cost-effective in-house alternatives based on commonalities and interoperability between existing and future technology. This 36-months cross-border project will gather 21 Entities from 13 European Countries, more than half of which were already part of the FIRES project.

title: Technologies for existing and Future MBTs
objective: The use of the Main Battle Tank (MBT) in military operations represents one of the most important innovations in modern warfare. Undoubtedly, the MBT has been key to battle superiority in particular when using technological advantages in novel and disruptive ways. However, for the last decades the MBT has mainly seen small step enhancements while lethality and multitude of threats from low to high tech have increased dramatically and the potential battlespace environment has widened to include expeditionary and closer to home operations. In order for the MBT to effectively reach in the future the desired military objectives, the role, characteristics and tactical utilisation of the MBT within operations have to be modernised, and keep pace with battlespace evolution and digitisation, using technologies efficiently and effectively to provide a coherent Land Systems Battle force. To achieve this objective, FMBTech aims to develop a modular design for agile, adaptive, intelligent and cooperative current/new/future MBT systems, able to deliver cutting edge Through Life Capabilities (TLC). These Capabilities will be based on a variety of state-of-the-art Technology Bricks that will provide seamless functional services across agile integrated modular open scalable architectures for the MBT as the centre of a Digitised Capabilities Envelope, giving enhanced ability to interface with unmanned systems and increased crew efficiency thanks to AI. This approach lends itself to commonality and interchangeability of equipment across the battle force offering coherent capabilities, and is supported by a solid Model Based Systems Engineering process Preliminary Design. Case Studies for current/new/future MBTs with simulations advocated by Business Cases will ensure feasibility of a coherent roadmap of sustainable development for EU defence TLC MBTs, as well as early de-risking the Next Generation of MBTs and potential early exploitation of disruptive technologies in current MBTs.

title: Main ARmoured Tank of Europe
objective: The MARTE project addresses the need for greater European defence autonomy and superiority by addressing the crucial area of the Main Battle Tank (MBT) where many EU Member States (MS) operate outdated MBT models. Key objectives of MARTE are to: (1) Study and design of a new European MBT platform as well consideration of technology upgrades for current MBTs altogether addressing all future threats and needs, operating in diverse environments, offering superior protection, detection, and firepower; (2) Increase the competitive advantage of European companies in the worldwide MBT industry, fostering economic growth and high-tech job creation; (3) Reinforce EU Defence Technological and Industrial Base autonomy, reduce dependence on third-country innovations focusing on European technologies ; (4) Foster cross-border cooperation, involving 15 SMEs and 4 Mid-Caps; (5) Enhance cost-effectiveness and lifecycle efficiency compared to existing MBT solutions; (6) Further contribute to the integration of the European defence market with the involvement of 49 entities and support from 11 MS and Norway. MARTE will be implemented through six work packages. The project will begin with CONOPS and System requirements, followed by technology studies and an evaluation. System and subsystem specifications will be performed and completed with the system and subsystem design description. System Architecture will be designed, resulting in the Preliminary Design Review as the final output of the project. MARTE will be efficiently managed thanks to a dedicated company (MARTE ARGE), a well-defined methodology, identified milestones and KPIs. The consortium aims to achieve project objectives within 24 months, with an EDF support of 20M€. MARTE's innovative approach and large-scale European collaboration will pave the way to the market introduction of the new MBT by 2035.

title: Maintenance, Joining, and Repair innovation in multidomain defence
objective: MaJoR is a development project to enable and advance joining, maintenance and repair technologies from Europe's R&T, industry base and SMEs and achieve up to TRL6 through demonstrator activities in three defence domains – air, sea and land. The project addresses the need to expand the EU defence industry's capability and secure European autonomy through cross-border and multi-domain collaboration. Each domain will develop a platform representing the test environment for various technologies developed within MaJoR. For the air domain, a fuselage of a UAV, also known as a Remote Carrier, will be prototyped, the sea domain platform comprises a lightweight, integrated ship mast structure, and lastly, the land platform will be a hybrid truck cabin made of fibre reinforced plastic (FRP). In total, seven technology streams are defined: Bonding and welding system development for composite and multi-materials, Material modelling and simulation/design and sizing method, Integrated structural design incl. integrated functions, Assembly process technology addressing manufacturing scalability, SHM - Inspection and monitoring, Multi-material repairs incl. composites, and Metallic repairs. Furthermore, MaJoR shall empower Europe's SMEs and start-ups with the FSTP programme by providing them financial support (max. €60k per candidate, ≤60 in total) and technical/entrepreneurship mentoring to bring their technologies to the European defence market. MaJoR consortium comprises 35 beneficiaries and one affiliated partner, covering ten member states, and a total grant of €30 million. Through the development and application of the technologies in MaJoR, the project aims to bring down assembly cost by 40%, save 20% weight, increase production speed by 30%, accelerate the design process by 30%, increase 10% more space for systems, reduce the radar cross section (RCS) signature and increase vehicle availability, thereby strengthening the mission-readiness of EU defence.

title: Next Generation Military Integrated Modular Avionics
objective:
In the foreseeable future, a new generation of military aerial platforms demands advanced avionics supporting innovative architectures, enhanced functionalities, and heightened performance, safety, and cyber resilience. Operating through a system-of-systems approach, these platforms need increased data sharing and processing, alongside specific requirements for development cycles and defense-related missions. 

The specific objective of this proposal is to study, design and demonstrate, within a 36-months timeframe, key components for the next generation of modular avionics, including:

- Identification of needs related to next generation military integrated modular avionics which will be used by legacy and next generation aerial platforms. 
- Study and identification of new high level architecture principles that should consider the need of multi-domain operations (from a safety perspective) and multi-level security.
- Identification, study and proof of concept of key technologies which must support possible future architecture solutions considering multiple constraints.
- Design of NG-MIMA scalable reference architecture(s) to be instantiated on different platforms.

NG-MIMA aims to revolutionize military avionics by bringing a new era of integrated modular avionics carefully tailored to address the distinctive and evolving requirements of military airborne platforms. Pioneering a novel European-scale design, the consortium emphasizes collaboration, harmonization, and integration with other initiatives (such as EICACS (EDF-2021-AIR-D-CAC)). The resulting NG-MIMA architecture and technologies will pave the foundation for future actions such as a full demonstrator or even the seed of a future European standard. Led by INDRA, the well-balance consortium comprises 26 entities from 10 EU Member States. NG-MIMA is poised to significantly contribute to European sovereignty and nurturing a robust ecosystem for military integrated modular avionics in defense.

OFFICE NATIONAL D'ETUDES ET DE RECHERCHES AEROSPATIALES

title: OPTIcal MilitAry Secure communications
objective:
OPTIMAS will develop a robust free-space optical (FSO) communication solution for multi-domain defence applications, showcasing one of the most challenging demonstration cases for optical communications: the link between a UAV and a satellite with an astounding Gbps data rate, analysing the future incorporation of QKD capability.
OPTIMAS will define, design, develop and test a prototype for airborne laser communication system (ALCoS) applicable to different satellite constellations (LEO, MEO and GEO) to be integrated into Air, Naval, C5ISR center and Land units. The OPTIMAS demonstrator will focus on three communication subsystems for secure and high-speed bidirectional communication: 1) A LEO satellite with accurate Pointing Acquisition and Tracking (PAT) function 2) Key enabling technologies for FSO communications 3) analysis to further include Quantum key distribution (QKD).
During its 3.5-year duration, OPTIMAS will bring into service optical communications between satellite and UAVs, demonstrate scalability and interoperability in other domains, achieve demonstrable data rates of air and satellite link in the order of Gbps, ensure beam compensation related to atmospheric conditions and divergence generated by the atmosphere, and integrate into  RPAS, easily adaptable to MALE RPAS.
OPTIMAS relies on a balanced consortium of 12 beneficiaries from 7 European countries, built on the close interaction between large companies, 5 innovative SMEs, 3 mid-caps, and 3 research organisations. With its disruptive potential, in terms of transmission speed, miniaturisation of onboarded communication solutions, piezoelectric actuation, stability/agility/pointing and QKD integration, OPTIMAS will be a game changer for ultra-secure high-bandwidth communications in warfare and battle zones.

title: SEabed and Anti-submarine warfare Capability through Unmanned featuRe for Europe
objective:
European Naval Forces are going to face new threats such as proliferation of submarines, unmanned systems, heavy/light/very light weight torpedoes, hyper-fast torpedoes. It introduces new operational theatres, requires new capabilities and new concepts of operation. Anti-submarine warfare and Seabed warfare are more than ever two topics of utmost importance for the EU and its security
European navies can no longer deal with this evolution of threats without new technology and systems specifically designed for them. However, they need to experiment at sea in order to refine their operational concepts and get confidence in this new technology before launching long and expensive acquisition programs. This is exactly the overall purpose of SEACURE EDF project to develop, demonstrate at sea and evaluate an integrated system of unmanned platforms to successfully perform joint ASW and SBW operations to protect critical maritime infrastructure, ready to join conventional Task Forces. 

The key innovation of this project is to base the development on an open and agile architecture, interoperable using NATO standards, modular and scalable. The solution will be composed of a command, control and communication system, a set of maritime unmanned systems (aerial, surface and underwater), along with their dedicated payloads and effectors. The aim will be to reach at least a maturity level equivalent to technology readiness level 6. One of the main challenges being to bring the navigation autonomy and mission autonomy at this level and reduce the number of operators. 

In the future the system developed in SEACURE could be proposed in various configurations and extended to other missions (ISR, Anti surface warfare, Mine Warfare). Its ambition is to enable EU navies to deal with numerous simultaneous missions in larger areas of operations. It will leverage from the synergies of all the consortium members to achieve this overarching goal.

NEDERLANDSE ORGANISATIE VOOR TOEGEPAST NATUURWETENSCHAPPELIJK ONDERZOEK TNO

title: Autonomous Rapid Recognition Operation Warship
objective:
Autonomous Rapid Recognition Operation Warship (ARROW)

The ARROW hydrofoil prototype will answer the critical and technological challenges of modern maritime combat. This faster and safer Unmanned Surface Vehicle (USV) will address some current technical capability gaps experienced by the EU navies, increasing sailing stability and autonomy in particular in tumultuous high seas. The autonomous systems managing both boat navigation and operations, combined with an hydrofoil system, will represent a major technological breakthrough.
keywords: Autonomous; Warship; Foil; Carbon hull; Recognition Operations; 12m; USV; technological breakthrough

title: Single end-user Device for Secure Command, Control and Communication (SD4SC3)
objective:
End-user devices like tablets and smartphones are becoming more and more important in modern military operations around the globe. Most of these end-user devices however are neither rugged nor secure enough for use in increasingly complex military environments, involving different mission-classifications, and facing constant cyber- as well as electronic warfare threats.
In addition, most of these end-user devices are manufactured outside of Europe. Given the increasing dependency on digital battlefield information this must be considered an enormous strategic risk.
In order to become truly independent, Europe MUST create its own INDEPENDENT and INNOVATIVE military computer hardware industry!
The Consortium behind project BATTLEPAD is made up of three SME-companies from three EU member states (Austria, Croatia, Slovenia) and wants to be an integral part of that industry, designing innovative end-user devices for military applications built on real-world use-cases
The overall idea is to integrate a Software Defined Radio (SDR) into an already existing, cyber-secure and rugged end-user device (EUD), currently at TRL Level 6/7, MADE IN AUSTRIA, providing for the first time, a fully integrated edge computing and communication platform for secure and future-proof digitalization of Armed Forces including enhanced networked function capabilities to also get the upper hand over Electronic Warfare – in other words: The digital “Swiss Army Knife” for the soldier.
Based on the Initial Harmonized Defence Capability Requirements (IHCDR) signed by the three participating member states, this DEVELOPMENT ACTION is aiming at disrupting the EUD as well as the tactical radio industry, creating something entirely new.

title: GNSS-free navigation and geolocation of objects based on satellite imagery maps and other sensor data
objective: The BadB project addresses the critical need for resilient and robust navigation systems by developing a novel GNSS-free navigation solution. Global Navigation Satellite Systems (GNSS) have become integral to modern navigation, but their vulnerabilities to signal disruptions, jamming, and spoofing pose significant risks. This topic has become timely due to the importance of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) and Unmanned Ground Vehicles (UGVs) in current and future battlefields. While gunfire from the ground accounts for some, the UAVs are going down in droves due to electronic warfare systems of the enemy forces. While bigger UAVs are already equipped with GNSS-free navigation systems, the technology is too heavy and too expensive to be implemented in small UAV systems. To mitigate these challenges, BadB has assembled a consortium of experts in the field of UAV development, artificial Intelligence, software development and robotics, with a general objective to develop a novel GNSS-free navigation system for small UAV systems. The main aim is to develop navigation solutions for land and aerial vehicles without relying on GNSS. Specific objectives include the development of weather independent up-to-date satellite imagery maps for UAVs and UGVs, development of a machine vision module, an image recognition system and development of a path planning system, based on sensor data, cross-platform data sharing and swarming. The consortium will meet these challenges and provide a feasible GNSS-free navigation solution that can be implemented in the navigation systems in small UAVs and UGVs. The expected impact of the project is for European defence forces and the defence industry to have an affordable, platform-agnostic GNSS-free navigation solution for unmanned systems. The mission success rate and quality are improved by using multiple UAVs and UGVs in the single mission with a single operator.

title: A Man-Portable vertical take-off ISTAR UAV system
objective:
The contemporary military landscape is rapidly evolving, necessitating advancements in Unmanned Aerial Vehicles to enhance the capabilities of North Atlantic Treaty Organization forces. Despite significant strides in UAV technology, there remains considerable potential for enhancement, particularly in the domain of single soldier portable UAVs.
The recent events caused by the Russian aggression to Ukraine have underlined how trench warfare is still an essential part of military operation. When the operations are performed in hard to reach and urban environment, handheld laser designation systems are often used to provide precision strike capabilities. A better, safer approach would be to use a man-portable UAV with laser designation capability instead. However, usually these systems are expensive, complex, heavy, and only larger platforms have the sufficient payload capacity to include the target designation capability. The losses of drones in the Ukraine war highlight that expensive platforms aren't universally effective. Instead, their application is restricted to specific operational scenarios, and a broader implementation runs the risk of being unsustainable. On the other hand, the operational advantages that unmanned platforms can provide are very well established.
To provide a new solution to the issue, Origin Robotics (Latvia) together with consortium partners Aktyvus Photonics UAB (Lithuania) and LEOSYS Laser and Electro – Optic Systems GmbH (Germany) plan to develop MPortISTAR, a mini (less than 15kg), man-portable vertical take-off UAV with integrated laser target designator, compatible with standard NATO laser-guided precision munition. The platform will demonstrate cost-effectiveness, amounting to only one-fifth of the expenses associated with large UAVs. This makes it capable of assuming target acquisition tasks from larger UAVs at a significantly reduced cost.

participants.name: LEOSYS LASER AND ELECTRO-OPTIC SYSTEMS GMBH

title: A Man-Portable vertical take-off ISTAR UAV system
objective:
The contemporary military landscape is rapidly evolving, necessitating advancements in Unmanned Aerial Vehicles to enhance the capabilities of North Atlantic Treaty Organization forces. Despite significant strides in UAV technology, there remains considerable potential for enhancement, particularly in the domain of single soldier portable UAVs.
The recent events caused by the Russian aggression to Ukraine have underlined how trench warfare is still an essential part of military operation. When the operations are performed in hard to reach and urban environment, handheld laser designation systems are often used to provide precision strike capabilities. A better, safer approach would be to use a man-portable UAV with laser designation capability instead. However, usually these systems are expensive, complex, heavy, and only larger platforms have the sufficient payload capacity to include the target designation capability. The losses of drones in the Ukraine war highlight that expensive platforms aren't universally effective. Instead, their application is restricted to specific operational scenarios, and a broader implementation runs the risk of being unsustainable. On the other hand, the operational advantages that unmanned platforms can provide are very well established.
To provide a new solution to the issue, Origin Robotics (Latvia) together with consortium partners Aktyvus Photonics UAB (Lithuania) and LEOSYS Laser and Electro – Optic Systems GmbH (Germany) plan to develop MPortISTAR, a mini (less than 15kg), man-portable vertical take-off UAV with integrated laser target designator, compatible with standard NATO laser-guided precision munition. The platform will demonstrate cost-effectiveness, amounting to only one-fifth of the expenses associated with large UAVs. This makes it capable of assuming target acquisition tasks from larger UAVs at a significantly reduced cost.

participants.name: LEOSYS LASER AND ELECTRO-OPTIC SYSTEMS GMBH

title: SATELLITE DETECTION, ANALYSIS AND MEASUREMENT SYSTEM FOR PROACTIVE AND REACTIVE DEFENCE
objective:
"In modern armies, there is a need to provide tools and sensors that analyze and/or, in many cases, have the capability to be aware of enemy communication, to degrade or deny the access to electromagnetic spectrum in the contested space environment using ground-based platforms. 
The main objective of SDAM4PRD is to provide the user a system with the necessary intelligence and information to be aware of the space communication of the adversary, and that will facilitate the optimal strategy and means on how to degrade or disrupt an adversary’s satellite communications in a reversible way. In addition, the system provide mechanism to recognize if friendly communications are being attacked. It allows operators to deny the capability for as long as it is needed before returning the satellite to its previous status. The execution of the action to be taken will be always approved and executed by proper military officer(s). The final objective is therefore to protect the interests of the European Union and our Armed Forces in a specific area. 

The SDAM4PRD system arises from the need of the military to have not only surveillance and monitoring capacity ""SDA"" of the spatial domain, but have a capacity to influence this domain ""Space Control"". The SDAM system is designed for GEO, MEO and LEO satellite communications, including platforms within BLOS as long as their radiant systems can track targets in the frequency bands for which it is designed.

The main feature of SDAM4PRD that makes it exclusive and only a few military powers have it, is its ability to analyze different operational scenarios by obtaining data from its own advanced monitoring system together with information processed (data fusion) from other OSINT and HUMINT sources.

The scope of the project will include the design, implementation and validation of a deployable portable infrastructure (military vehicle), and a fixed platform that will collect the information and train the system."

participants.name: EXUS SOFTWARE MONOPROSOPI ETAIRIA PERIORISMENIS EVTHINIS

title: Rotary Suspension for Armoured Vehicles 2
objective:
This project’s aim is to fully validate, qualify and increase the efficiency across the lifecycle of a fully rotary hydropneumatic suspension comprised by the merging of a rotary damper (energy dissipating element) and a rotary pneumatic spring (elastic component). It’s born as the natural continuation of the Project SRB funded under Grant nº. EDIDP-SME-2020-064-SRB, which ends after the initial prototype testing phase in Nov/2024, having so far successfully completed all milestones.
Rotary suspension technologies fit particularly well on tracked vehicles due to their compactness, robustness, and incomparable energy dissipated/space claim ratio. In the past, the constant increase in weight and high–mobility requirements have challenged the different suspension technologies.
Rotary dampers have notably increased their energy dissipation capabilities by moving from friction-based technologies to Full Hydraulic high efficiency/low maintenance rotary dampers.
As opposed to current solutions (hydropneumatics cylinders, torsion bar with mechanic or hydraulic damper, rocker actuated rotary suspension systems based on hydropneumatics cylinders) this system would be able to offer a much better performance as well as a much easier integrability. Its suspension travel to space claim required ratio will, whilst maintaining high force damping, ensure a much better vehicle mobility, performance, and product integrability. Another added advantage of the system, will be the possibility to regulate the vehicle ride height, ensuring it is always tailored to the vehicle’s specific need. The final system will also be able to operate under aggressive environments and extremely harsh temperature conditions without detriment to the system’s performance.
The current project shall cover studies, design in order to include major improvement from the result of SRB, testing, qualification and increasing efficiency so as to guarantee a cost-effective, easily implementable, result of the action.

title: Space Threats Analysis based on Automated reaL-time In-situ capabilities and Onboard processing decentralized Network
objective:
"As the EU increasingly relies on Space Infrastructure to deliver its services for the public, institutions and governments, so are the level of threats toward its satellites. Space is now a strategic domain for Europe: services such as Galileo, providing worldwide positioning, are also essential for the banking system, and governmental communications are secured through trusted operators (LuxGovSat) or dedicated infrastructure (IRIS²). New threats will take the form of space-to-space capabilities requiring In-Situ assets protection and reactive identification & threat characterization. European Space is ""congested and contested” and requires advanced protection capabilities.
As a Development Action, STAALION (Space Threats Analysis based on Automated reaL-time In-situ capabilities and Onboard processing decentralized Network), aims at demonstrating the potential performances of In-Situ Space Domain Awareness. The expected system and development action should cover:
1.	New generation platform LEO & GEO fitted for demanding on-board processing power and agile mobility in space 
2.	On-board advanced detection & anomaly identification processes
3.	Space based multi-modal processing for the characterization of threats"

title: Advancing Robust and Creative Human language technologies through CHallenge Events and Research
objective:
Human Language Technologies (HLT) development has boomed in recent years. These developments have yielded significant improvements in machine translation capabilities, automatic speech processing, extraction of information of interest from texts, etc. They have facilitated the operational use of HLT in several information systems, including online translation services, call centres, and voice assistants. 
However, the application and the deployment of these technologies in Defence systems for C4ISR and multinational operations reveals a number of shortcomings, failing to meet the operational needs and to achieve the required accuracy. This is mainly due to the specific needs of the Defence domain and its corresponding characteristics (dialects, low resources, or rare languages, use in the field, noise and communications quality, etc.) which are not taken into account by solutions originating in the civilian domain. With the digitalisation of the battlefield, which leads to more and more complex user interfaces and to ever-increasing volumes of language data to process, there is a growing need for enhanced language processing capabilities, particularly in the areas of speech recognition, machine translation, and natural language processing.
To both tackle these problems and preserve Europe sovereignty in the sector, ARCHER will deliver a multi-annual challenge encouraging research to advance HLT bricks specifically tailored to Defence applications. The challenge will serve as a pivotal resource for evaluating the efficiency and reliability of HLT bricks, to best meet end-user needs.
keywords: Human Language HLT NLP Translation Speech Challenge

title: Language Innovations: Nimble, Guided, User-friendly, Agile and Robust Solutions Eliminating Defense Challenges
objective: LINGUARISE-DC embodies a transformative initiative to address the critical need for advanced language processing in the European defence sector. By leveraging cutting-edge Human Language Technologies (HLT), the project aims to enhance communication proficiency, intelligence gathering, and collaboration within ad-hoc coalitions, ultimately contributing to the success of EU-led missions. The modular and adaptable nature of the proposed platform positions it as a groundbreaking solution with the potential to revolutionize language capabilities in military operations.

title: NEMO (laNguagE Modules develOpment) will develop a demonstrator of a variety of Human Language Technologies (HLTs) tailored on the needs of Defence, especially user-driven adaptation.
objective:
NEMO will develop a system to perform a variety of Human Language Technologies (HLTs) including speech recognition,
handwritten and printed documents recognition,keywords spotting, information retrieval, semantic analysis, Named Entity Recognition (NER), summarization, and translation to all official EU languages. The system will have the form of a demonstrator and will be tailored on the needs of Defence. These functionalities will be adapted to the Defence domain enabling them to deal with military-specific vocabulary and related semantics.

The NEMO demonstrator will enable users to adapt them using their own data or by interactively providing supervision, with an approach based on LLM, fine-tuned with data provided by end-user. The performance evaluation metrics will automatedly be computed and each module will explain its outputs and offer assisted annotation services. the NEMO demonstrator will be able to run locally and will offer visualization services and visual analytics. The NEMO demonstrator will take part in the Technology Challenges organized within the HLTO call every year.

UNIVERSITATEA NATIONALA DE APARARE CAROL I

title: Aerodynamically Controlled Thrust ORientation for enhanced manoeuvrability in counter UAS and future advanced defence applications
objective:
A technological innovation known as thrust vectoring (TV) has emerged first for rockets in 1930s and then for combat aircrafts. Unlike conventional control surfaces, TV dynamically manipulates the direction of engine exhaust gases, increasing maneuverability, stability, and control authority for various aerospace applications, playing a crucial role in a defence context. The replacement of conventional aerodynamic control surfaces brings enormous advantages such as low-observability, reduction of aircraft mass and zero-lift drag.  When using TV, modern fighter jets rely on Mechanical Vectoring (MV) by changing the geometry of the nozzle, but MV comes with severe disadvantages: complexity, extra weight and maintenance, increased radar cross section... 

ACTOR will investigate a promising solution: the Fluidic Thrust Vectoring (FTV) which is a method of controlling the direction of thrust in aircraft and rocket engines using fluid dynamics principles rather than mechanical means. FTV retains the advantages of MTV without the need of the complex hardware of the variable geometry devices. The FTV technology does not significantly increase the aircraft weight, and exhaust system integration into the vehicle is simple. But despite these advantages, FTV is a low TRL technology, leading to some limitations, uncertainties and challenges reducing the potential for its applications in a defence context. 

The ACTOR project gathers 6 partners from 3 EU countries to tackle these challenges by increasing the knowledge about FTV concepts in subsonic regime, down select the ones that have the higher potential in terms of applications, tests them in wind tunnel facilities and by CFD simulations, and make one step towards concrete applications, focusing on a Counter- Unmanned Aircraft Systems application, and first test on a turbojet engine bench. The workplan, covering 42 months, is structured such as each of the work packages encompasses one type of activity with clear milestones.

title: Disruptive Electromagnetic 70 mm Rocket System
objective:
DEMAROCK is a collaborative research project between Ytsab Defence, Thales Belgium and Royal Netherlands Aerospace Centre, Netherland to design a ground-breaking Electromagnetic Launcher for Improved 70mm Rockets. 
The ultimate goal of DEMAROCK is to elevate launch capabilities to an unprecedented level, offering a host of innovative advantages that will reshape the landscape of rocket launches.
The Electromagnetic Launcher is a revolutionary piece of technology that will redefine the way rockets are launched. Unlike traditional methods that rely on the combustion of chemical propellants to generate the necessary thrust, this electromagnetic launcher will employ electromagnetic forces to propel the rocket into the skies. This innovative approach will not only significantly reduce the reliance on conventional rocket propellants but also enhance precision and control during the launch phase.
One of the key innovations of this project is the timing mechanism incorporated into the rocket design. Extensive research and studies will be undertaken to optimize and determine the ideal ignition time after the electromagnetic launch that will result in the rocket achieving its maximum potential distance. By conducting these in-depth investigations, the project will define the perfect ignition moment that will propel the rocket to its farthest possible reach. This ingenious feature will eliminate the need for a substantial amount of starting fuel, which is typically required to initiate the rocket's flight. By doing so, DEMAROCK will make rocket launches more efficient, cost-effective, and more environmentally friendly, further solidifying its status as a trailblazing initiative in the realm of rocket technology. 
DEMAROCK will also study an entirely new rocket design. The consortium will explore innovative materials, aerodynamic configurations, and propulsion systems to create a rocket that seamlessly complements the electromagnetic launcher's capabilities.

participants.name: STICHTING KONINKLIJK NEDERLANDS LUCHT - EN RUIMTEVAARTCENTRUM

title: LAser CEramics
objective:
The use of high peak power Ultrashort Pulse Lasers with High Average Power (USP-HAP) as a potentially disruptive LDEW component
systems is increasingly attracting attention worldwide the possibility of accessing new regimes of atmospheric propagation and new
target interaction effects related to the ultrashort pulse energy delivery, with unique foreseen electromagnetic, mechanical and
thermal effects linked to both the peak power and the average delivered laser power. The combination of peak and average power
foreseen for these and other crucial applications requires the development of a novel laser technology exploiting direct diode
pumping of scalable gain media capable of broadband amplification that can be customized in shape and doping profile and can
have a controllable delivery lead time.
In our LACE project we aim at addressing production of novel transparent ceramics as laser gain media to finally empower
development of this new class of laser. Indeed, production of laser-grade ceramics is a challenging and complex task, and often
linked to proprietary processes requiring dedicated approaches for the specific selected materials. Current availability of laser
ceramic materials is extremely limited, with very few manufacturers capable of producing and delivering samples for research
purposes. Based on the existing strong expertise on ceramic development in the EU and fully included in this project, we aim at 1)
establishing capability of production of high-performance, laser-grade transparent ceramics of broadband laser gain materials based
on Thulium for chirped pulse amplification and 2) developing advanced multilayer custom coatings needed for delivering fully
operational ceramics laser gain media.
We strongly believe that the proposed developments are strategic to fill the existing technological gaps to reach maturity in USP-
HAP laser technology in Europe and to push the TRL of LDEW systems based on this technology closer to a prototype level.

CONSORCIO PARA EL DISENO, CONSTRUCCION, EQUIPAMIENTO Y EXPLOTACION DEL CENTRO DE LASERES PULSADOS ULTRACORTOS ULTRAINTENSOS

title: Sustainable Water Innovations for Fielded Troops
objective: In the dynamic landscape of military operations, logistical support to combat units remains a persistent challenge, with water emerging as a resource-intensive asset, critical for diverse needs (e.g., consumption, hygiene, equipment maintenance). Traditional approaches in field water management employ linear, single-use models that lack efficient and reliable procedures and technologies for alternative sourcing and recycling, thus imposing significant operational and logistical constraints. SWIFT offers a disruptive approach to water management through technological innovations for modular field camps suitable for deployed military units within expeditionary and European defence contexts. Specifically, the project will concentrate on exploring the potential of emerging water reuse concepts and technologies, including, but not restricted to, graphene-based treatment technologies and advanced photocatalytic processes, to provide clean and safe water for military personnel in various (often harsh) operational environments. The project also addresses the issue of energy demands for these novel technologies, identifying opportunities to optimally couple water and energy systems, enabling demand side management capabilities, thus resulting to increased efficiency and resilience for both. SWIFT directly confronts and develops innovations for additional relevant challenges, such as health risk assessment and disruptive safety monitoring for troops that make use of the proposed circular water systems, by developing new tools to monitor and manage health risks originating from water reuse and external contamination threats. SWIFT will develop AI-based Smart Camp infrastructure Planning Agents for the design and Digital Twin technologies for operating the treatment innovations of the project, aiming to significantly boost the ability of camp planners to better design, monitor and control safe water systems and services under a wide range of operational contexts and challenges.
keywords: Autonomous water cycle management for deployed units;Water treatment & reuse innovations;Militarization of Environmental Technologies;Health & Safety;Digital Twin & AI Technologies;Logistical Support

participants.name: EQUIPOS MOVILES DE CAMPANA ARPA SA

title: Command, Control, and Communications for Multi-X Swarms
objective:
The Command, Control, and Communications for Multi-X Swarms (SWARM-C3) project studies and designs radically new concepts and disruptive approaches for the effective management of robotic swarms in military operations. The multi-X swarms are designed to be 1) utilized in operations involving multiple physical terrestrial military domains (i.e., multi-domain: land, sea, and air), 2) commanded from multiple locations, 3) controlled at multiple system levels, and 4) supervised and commanded by multiple human operators in different roles. 

From the technological perspective, the SWARM-C3 designs are enablers for future command and control and decision-support functions for multi-X swarms, the related human-machine interfaces, robust data communications for the swarms, and autonomous multi-domain swarming solutions.

The main objective of SWARM-C3 is to develop a novel Concept of Operations for the management of multi-X swarms of autonomous uncrewed systems. Novel military swarming scenarios will be used as a working method in SWARM-C3 and the scenarios will be defined together with defense experts to ground the planned design and simulation work in the project to realistic situations. 

SWARM-C3 provides ground-breaking solutions and approaches to the European defense sector:
1) Beyond state-of-the-art swarming to increase the predictability, reliability, and trustworthiness of swarms;
2) Human-centered radical innovation design coupled with high ethical standards to enable efficient human-swarm interaction and effective command and control; and
3) Cutting-edge innovations in ultra-secure data communications technologies for interference-tolerant swarm communications solutions.

In the future, the different innovative design solutions from SWARM-C3 can be integrated to a holistic C3 product. A major beneficial long-term outcome of the project will be a change and re-evaluation of military operations from the multi-X swarms perspective to gain defense superiority.

title: INNOVATIVE AI-ENHANCED, REMOTELY POWERED, INDIRECT FIRE OBSERVATION SYSTEM UTILIZING UNMANNED VEHICLES
objective:
"Indirect fire support is a crucial component of modern military operations, primarily involving the use of artillery, mortars, rockets, and other indirect fire weapons to engage and neutralize enemy targets that are not within the direct line of sight of the firing unit. Unlike direct fire, where weapons are aimed directly at visible targets, indirect fire relies on the projection of projectiles on a trajectory that allows them to reach targets that may be beyond the observer's line of sight or hidden behind obstacles, such as hills, buildings, or forests. Indirect fire support is typically used to strike at distant or concealed enemy positions, fortifications, and areas of strategic importance, making it a fundamental element of combined arms warfare. Indirect fire support observation is the critical process of acquiring, relaying, and utilizing information about enemy targets and the effectiveness of indirect fire support on those targets. Indirect fire support observation is usually conducted by human observers on the ground, known as forward observers. These observers serve as the ""eyes and ears"" of the artillery units, ensuring that fires are delivered accurately and effectively while also contributing valuable information for overall battlefield management and decision-making. Yet, there are several potential disadvantages to having a physical person acting as observer for indirect firing, including vulnerability, communication, limited field of view and cost. Overall, while observers can provide valuable information to artillery units, their use also comes with significant risks and limitations. VICTORIOUS proposes a Next Generation UxV-enabled observation system, having as its main scope the artillery/indirect fire support. The ultimate goal of VICTORIOUS is to be a useful tool, which will allow defence stakeholders to locate and be informed in a timely manner of the exact location and nature of events occurring in the battlefield, by using disruptive technologies.
The main objectives of VICTORIOUS project include:
•	To design a disruptive Next Generation Indirect fire observation system, enhanced by innovative AI, UAVs, UGV, Sensors, Wireless Power and data transfer technological solutions, based on the existing military operations processes and procedures, enhancing the capabilities and effectiveness of fire support military tactical operations.
•	To investigate and design disruptive means of wireless Power & Data Transfer
•	To engage blockchain technologies in Fire Support Operations
•	To design innovative AI tools and algorithms to support Indirect Fire Observation operations
•	To employ various sensors and automations for target acquisition
•	To employ UxVs and supporting devices for Indirect Fire Observation operations
•	To provide an integrated fire support observation system and to verify it under realistic scenarios.

VICTORIOUS is an innovative system which proposes a combination of radical technological breakthroughs for addressing the crucial aspect of firing observation. These technologies include:
•	Unmanned Vehicles deployed on the battlefield, for improved situational awareness using cooperative perception and AI
•	Wireless power transfer from ground to UAVs for 24/7 operational capability
•	Wireless non-intercepted data communication between ground and UAVs
•	AI algorithms for threat detection, identification, classification and acquisition on the EDGE
•	Block Chain technologies for creating a security bubble for VICTORIOUS assets
•	Training of the observers based on innovative learning technologies
•	Innovative UI design including the whole subsystems integration & API in order to connect to existing systems."

title: A portable, private, high-performance, A portable, private, high-performance, secure, robust, resilient and rapidly deployed ad-hoc tactical 5G and beyond communications system for military operations
objective: Modern warfare requires the delivery of secure, prompt and decisive action on the battlefield based on real-time high-speed data. Military communication systems rely mostly on secure HF communications via the ionosphere during warfare conditions, however, existing HF systems cannot provide such capability due to limited bandwidth and other ionospheric conditions limitations. Additionally, the public communications telecom infrastructure will most probably be incapacitated by hostile enemy operations during warfare. 5G-MILNET proposes the design of a novel portable 5G-based tactical communications bubble, which can be rapidly and dynamically deployed on demand in the battlefield, to enable seamless, resilient and secure, high-speed, real-time connectivity and data flow across the battlefield and local backend control and command operations. 5G-MILNET will be designed as a cost-effective solution offering significant tactical advantages to EU military forces, including high portability, privacy, security, resiliency against RF jamming, and robustness avoiding single points of failure. By establishing such a system, war fighters can achieve more effective operations in communications-contested and denied environments and have access to data to perform their missions anywhere in the world (assuming close proximity of up to a few kilometers between the Command and Control Center and the battlefield) without the use of pre-existing communications infrastructure. This will be achieved through the use of small size 5G Software Defined Radio (SDR) micro cells that can be carried out by personnel as well as by military vehicles in the battlefield area.  The high-speed backhaul links with the headquarters can be established by the deployment of airborne 5G SDR units carried by a series of lightweight (graphene based) purpose-designed low altitude drones operated (semi) autonomously through swarm intelligence in strategic positions along the path.
