title: UnderwAter optical wireless communication network architecture empowered by adVanced opticAl materiaLs for sea bOrder protection and deep-sea exploratioN
objective: AVALON envisions the design of reliable and flexible, secure and private, scalable wireless system that supports high-speed communication (in the order of 10 Gbps to 100 Gbps) in beyond optical line of sight transmission distances that even reach 100 m, with almost zero-delay by taking advantage of the optical spectrum, pioneering technological advancements, such as metasurfaces for ensuring uninterrupted communication in obstacle riddled environments, and ML based design of medium access, resource allocation and network management techniques. For the realization of this vision, a novel system model will be developed and analyzed that will contain physical, medium access control (MAC) and resource management (RM) layer design considering the particularities of the optical band, a pioneering beyond Shannon theoretical framework will be extracted, and network ML methodologies that allow integrated and accurate communication and sensing will be proposed. These results are expected to “open the door” for the realization of novel highly capable underwater optical wireless networks for military applications
keywords: wireless optical communications; Optical Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces; Energy Efficiency;

GESELLSCHAFT FUR ANGEWANDTE MIKRO UND OPTOELEKTRONIK MIT BESCHRANKTERHAFTUNG AMO GMBH

title: Cyber-secure weArable, ultra-low-Power networked SensoRs for soldIer health monItoring.
objective: The CAPSARII project proposal aims at developing an innovative smart textile/wearable device and related ICT framework to display raw health data and Machine Learning (ML)-derived insights, designed specifically for military staff, which aligns with the concept of the Internet of Battlefield Things (IoBT), driven by Artificial Intelligence (AI) and edge computing, aiming to transform the monitoring of soldiers' psychophysical conditions and the evaluation of their performance, both in combat and training scenarios, thus allowing for real-time health status assessments through a specialized ML model. In the face of the challenges posed by an increasingly unstable world, the European Defence Agency has recently revised its Capability Development Plan (CDP) to reach new levels of preparedness and cooperation among the member countries. Among the 11 priorities identified in the CDP, improving ground combat capabilities requires enhancing the equipment of individual soldiers to obtain better protection. Fine grain-awareness of the physiological and psychological status of each soldier can be a key tool for making sound tactical decisions, as well as to provide life-saving information to medical support services. Wearable System Integration, Edge computing, Cloud-based analytics and AI Model Optimization for Resource Constrained Systems are among the key technological bricks at the foundation of the potential of disruption of CAPSARII project.

participants.name: BORN GMBH KNITWEAR FOR FASHION & ENGINEERING

title: CAmouflage THERmal INtelligent and Adaptive
objective:
CATHERINA (CAmouflage THERmo INteligent and Adaptive) will integrate optically adaptive camouflage with infrared and thermal detection-resistant properties to create a smart textile that will protect tomorrow's soldiers in the theatre of battle. Each characteristic bringing unique capabilities to create a holistic, groundbreaking textile solution towards a multi-level disguise, guaranteeing the safety of, among others, the soldiers. This integrated approach enhances its versatility and suitability for diverse defence scenarios. Unlike traditional modular camouflage systems that require manual adjustments and have limited responsiveness, this product combines various functions into one, simplifying deployment and optimizing resource allocation. Its ability to provide multi-level disguise is particularly valuable in life-or-death situations where camouflage is crucial. 

This collaboration of characteristics is the fulcrum on which the entirety of Project CATHERINA revolves, ensuring that each component's uniqueness is leveraged to its utmost potential, resulting in a harmonious and innovative blend that promises to reshape the landscape of the defence field.

The CATHERINA project, centred on innovation, prioritizes sustainable decision-making from the start, demonstrating its commitment to responsible innovation and ensuring the environmental and sustainable aspects of the end product throughout its duration. Making eco-conscious choices early in the project is imperative.

coordinator.name: GAHN LOGISTICA GESTION Y CONSULTORIA, SA

participants.name: GAHN LOGISTICA GESTION Y CONSULTORIA, SA
DEUTSCHE INSTITUTE FUR TEXTIL- UND FASERFORSCHUNG DENKENDORF

title: Design of Airborne Efficient and Disruptive Advanced front-end for Long range Unlimited Secure systems
objective:
DAEDALUS will develop technologies to provide the 5th & 6th generation fighters with SATCOM systems based on a disruptive cyber hardened RF front-end subsystem (applicable to EW, Datalink or RADAR subsystems). 
Existing bulky and vulnerable systems are not suitable, so research needs to focus on miniaturization and robustness of the electrical architecture; on the ultimate cybersecurity update demanded by the real imminence of effective quantum attacks; and on the need to ensure EU technological sovereignty. For this reason, DAEDALUS will research and validate technologies enabling the development of an efficient, robust, lightweight, miniaturized RF front end, that can be integrated into new, quantum resistant SATCOM systems that the new generation of multi-purpose combat fighters will carry. Technological innovations are:
- High efficiency and light weight dual Ka band miniaturization, by performing technological advances in GaN MMICs, innovative filtering, ultracompact power combining, topological optimization with FEM and additive manufacturing
- Enhanced vibration-induced phase noise resistance by new oscillator topologies and shock absorbing techniques to withstand high G vibrations
- Disruptive cooling and thermal design by ionic solution with corona effect and additive manufacturing
- RISC-V based optimized M&C, to enable strategical adoption of European-based processor architectures and customized HW acceleration of security features
- Post-Quantum (PC) cryptography and PC Blockchain for a realistic and efficient integration of RF links with Quantum Key Distribution infrastructure, to enable effective quantum safe communication and update of the air combat cloud (ACC) assets
DAEDALUS team (6 SME, 1 Industrial, 1 University and 1 from National Public sector) will also bring EDTIB autonomy and technological independence, by creating a fully EU supply chain for a superior SATCOM solution for ACCs to overcome current scenario, controlled by overseas companies.

title:
DEvelopMent of Enabling technologies for a THust-vectored hypersonic vehicle featuring innovative
combustoR and mAterials
objective:
Hypersonic weapons have the potential to revolutionize warfare and accelerate the achievement of European defence air superiority, especially in the current geo-political situation. In this scenario, supersonic combustion jet engines (scramjets) make it possible to achieve very high speeds at long ranges and with high flexibility. Actually, the scramjet technology is perceived as the key solution to cover large flight Mach number envelopes up to Mach 10-12 and even further. However, scramjets face many technical difficulties that need to be solved before they can enter regular service, in particular: 1) aerodynamic heating of the vehicle which at such high speeds becomes hardly manageable and undermines structural integrity; 2) reliable and efficient burning of the fuel, especially when transitioning from hydrogen to hydrocarbon-based fuels, which are more suitable for defence applications as they are safer, more storable and provide more readiness for deployment in the context of military applications; 3) the capability to change and control the thrust direction is key to promote tracking and interception of attacking threats, but at hypersonic flight speeds, the efficiency of conventional aerodynamic control surfaces is limited due to the shocks around the vehicle.
In DEMETHRA, three novel scramjet-enabling technologies will be investigated that can solve the above-mentioned main challenges: 1) innovative porous ceramics carbon-composite materials for transpiration cooling and fuel injection, 2) propellant injection and flame holding devices for hydrocarbon-based fuels and 3) thrust vectoring by means of jets and ramps in the nozzle. These technologies will be assessed using both numerical and experimental studies on a highly modular model that will be tested in a hypersonic wind tunnel laboratory, with the aim of de-risking potential future exploitation of the investigated technologies and provide an invaluable database for validation of proposed concepts.

participants.name: DEUTSCHES ZENTRUM FUR LUFT - UND RAUMFAHRT EV

title: European cybersecurity HSM/TPM device for securing micro/nano satellite and UAV communications
objective:
The main objective of the activity is to study and design an HSM/TPM device (Hardware Security Module /Trusted Platform Module), combined with a secure Operating System (Secure-OS) and attestation capabilities for securing the processing chain and data communications in Micro-satellites, Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAV) / Autonomous Surface Vehicles (ASV) and others. The HSM4COM solution will be capable of providing encryption, decryption and attestation functionalities.
The combined HW/SW device will constitute a generic highly customizable solution for both microsatellite and UAV platforms, being also applicable for use in ASV and other similar platforms. It will constitute a pure European solution, since it will be based on IP Cores developed in Europe to be used on existing FPGA chips.
The integration of an aerospace/defense secure-OS using an HSM/TPM as a trust anchor will create a unique and unparalleled solution in the sector. Our objective is to design a complete integrated environment, where together, the security-hardened OS and hardware based HSM/TPM provide an exceptional foundation, ensuring a robust and secure execution platform for applications in the aerospace/defence sectors.

title: Energy autonomous smart clothing to enhance soldier safety and connectivity in the battleground
objective:
Introduction:
The defence and security landscape are undergoing rapid changes. The EU is confronting new, multi-layered challenges and threats, and potential conflicts in its immediate vicinity, arising not only from unforeseen geopolitical developments but also from emerging technologies and new defence domains, such as cyber warfare. While the European continent has experienced a period of relative peace until recently, the dynamics shifted with Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine in February 2022 and the most recent conflict in Israel in the Middle East, disrupting the status quo and setting a new reality for the EU and its Member States (MS). Cyber warfare, misinformation campaigns, and the increasing use of drones and unmanned vehicles are reshaping the nature face of conflicts, necessitating continuous investment in new technologies and solutions to gain a technical, tactical and operational advantage against these evolving threats.
Despite critical technological advancements, including robots and unmanned systems, the dismounted soldier still remains an indispensable military asset. Therefore, significant advancements in dismounted soldier systems (DSS) are essential to maximize their effectiveness. Over the years, soldier systems have undergone significant development, particularly in incorporating new technological features to enhance operational capabilities and survivability. With a focus on modularity, interoperability and information superiority, state of the art soldier systems are capable of adapting to multiple operational parameters. They provide unparalleled information and communication capabilities, offer tactical information on the battlefield and can securely interface with various military systems in their vicinity. This approach is adopted by multiple commercial and national soldier systems solutions. National programmes such as the German IdZ-ES (Infanterist der Zukunft) programme and the French FELIN (Fantassin à Équipements et Liaisons Intégrés) programme serve as excellent examples of early strategic investments in new technologies and the advancement of soldier systems.
On the commercial side, several state-of-the-art soldier system solutions are being developed by leading defence companies, either through national programmes or independently. Examples include GLADIUS 2.0 by Rheinmetall (Germany), Team SABRE (Soldier, Augmentations and Battlefield Robotic Enablers) by Safran Electronics & Defence Australasia, BAE Systems Australia and Nova Systems Australia and New Zealand, among others. In all cases, modules undergo continuous development and enhancements, complemented by new sensors, technologies and devices, emphasizing the necessity for a hyper-connected, information and data-centric approach during operations. These systems are designed to provide soldiers a decisive advantage in modern combat, where situational awareness and lethality are crucial. 
In this framework, soldier systems are more dependent on power sources than ever before. This dependence arises from the increasing complexity and sophistication of soldier systems, which integrate a growing number of electronic devices. The demand for power sources is anticipated to escalate in the future, particularly with the integration of future technologies such as 5/6G connectivity and AI enhanced systems expected in the next decade. As operational energy requirements become more extensive and varied, it becomes imperative to adopt a comprehensive approach that addresses battlefield energy efficiency, robustness, security, and readiness. Various technologies will contribute to energy generation solutions, including solar cells, and fuel cells. Additionally, advanced storage options such as supercapacitors or high-power batteries will play a pivotal role, alongside sophisticated management and distribution systems like smart grids and predictive energy management models.
The advancement of soldier systems stands out as a priorit
y ac

title: rOcket dePloyed deEp Reconnaissance ANd Transmission system
objective:
Recent conflicts highlight the importance of fast-decision-making processes at every level of combat management. We identified a lack in digitalization and automatization on the lower tactical levels on the “last mile” of the battlefield. With OPERANT, we provide a solution to deploy a distributed acoustic sensing capability deep into the adversary domain (Observe). Although we use rocket deployment now, we highlight that the system design aims at withstanding high-g force allowing other harsh deployment methods. Upon detection of relevant information, the system autonomously calls for other components providing means to verify the information (e.g., visual identification). 

OPERANT system design includes a height-adjustable smart mast system combined with pan-tilt unit enabling line-of-sight supporting multiple sensor units as generic platform solution. The open electronical and mechanical interfaces qualify OPERANT’s components for multi-purpose multi-mission scenarios minimizing the logistic overhead coming with simultaneous use of different commercial systems. Communication benefits from distributed communication nodes with height-adjustable antennas spanning a mobile ad hoc network (MANet) including the observation point and a headquarter or a command post. Extractability and retractability of the mast minimize visual recon options during radio silence. Transporting information from an uncontrolled or contaminated area to a secure environment allows key personnel to concentrate on evaluation and analysis (Orient) without endangering them. 
With OPERANT, we uniquely access the deep adversary military territory and bring the information to the decision-maker (Decide). Putting the pieces together, we believe that OPERANT enables acceleration of the well-established combat operation’s OODA (Observe, Orient, Decide, Act)- loop applied during military campaigns.

participants.name: FRAUNHOFER GESELLSCHAFT ZUR FORDERUNG DER ANGEWANDTEN FORSCHUNG EV

title: Remote weAPon condition-based moniTORing System
objective:
While the extension of internet connections to military assets and the establishment of Internet of Military Things (IoMT) is gaining traction within the EU Defence Technological and Industrial Technology Base, yet existing IoT technology lacks the robustness and cost-effectiveness required to be routinely used in weapons. The project’s overall objective is to achieve predictive maintenance of firearms by co-designing and co-developing, with all participating and closely engaged end-users, tools, applications and technologies, constituting the low-cost Remote weAPon condition-based moniTORing System. RAPTOR proposes disruptive innovation such as: low cost robust, purpose-built printed sensors, bespoke miniature electronics, robust encrypted and secure wireless communication and the development of Artificial Intelligence (AI) algorithms for the processing of the data into useful and meaningful information (forecasting) for predictive maintenance alerting. This will enable the weapons’ operators to respond to critical mission operations in a more effective manner, to reduce the probabilities of weapons’ failure by achieving predictive maintenance and to increase their reliability and safety. The aim is to create a fully interoperable RAPTOR system with European Soldier Systems, achieved through the implementation of IoMT with embedded security by design, along the edge-cloud continuum.
RAPTOR innovations, component functionalities and performance will be validated through 3 Proof-of-Concept Test Cases: G3, GROT 762N, ALEX-338 in a representative environment (ballistic test site). In the long-term RAPTOR is projected to have a drastic impact on a wider range of weapons and weapon systems used by EU armed forces. The consortium is composed of 3 SMEs and 2 RTO/Academic beneficiary partners with vast experience in hardware electronics, printed sensors, ink materials, printed antennas, wireless communication, cybersecurity, software development, AI and edge computing.

title: Strengthening Defense Networks for the Quantum Era
objective: "SEQURED aims to fortify, future-proof, and enhance the resilience of defence systems and communication infrastructures against potential threats from quantum computing attacks. The project design a quantum-resistant cryptographic suite for data-sharing, processing, and secure storage. This suite will be resilient to quantum computing advancements and practical for integration into existing defence sector legacy systems, facilitating the transition to the post-quantum era. This will be achieved by developing specifications and implementing prominent quantum-resistant cryptographic algorithms and protocols, optimized for the defence sector’s strict timing and security requirements. The project will focus on implementing the four quantum-resistant algorithms selected by NIST: Kyber, Dilithium, FALCON, and SPHINCS+. It aims to provide complete specifications for their implementation, balancing sensitive security needs with optimal performance under time and power constraints. The design will also optimize processing power, considering military infrastructure characteristics and known vulnerabilities, particularly physical attacks. This involves developing a novel SW/HW co-design approach, allowing existing defence systems to adopt advanced quantum-resistant algorithms. Additionally, the project will explore the first-ever quantum-resistant trusted computing base tailored for defence applications. This adaptation will enable these systems to execute data sharing agreements through a quantum-resistant ledger with high security, even against physical attacks. Finally, the project will develop a cloud-based service, ""PQ-as-a-Service"", focusing on resource-constrained devices that cannot handle heavy cryptographic workloads, and a trusted path establishment service based on novel attestation mechanisms. This service will secure data transmission between classical and quantum-resistant nodes."

title: Swarm Technology for Enhanced Autonomous fLight and Tactical Handling
objective: STEALTH aims to contribute to the European Defense technological capacity of GNSS-free navigation in an uncertain or hostile air-space domain using Unmanned Autonomous Vehicles (UAV) in swarms. In an ever-shifting global security arena, we present a groundbreaking solution that fuses cutting-edge technologies i.e. the UAV GNSS-free autonomous navigation and swarms’ intelligence, to reshape the landscape of defense, drawing inspiration from real defense or even hybrid incidents occurred in recent conflicts, that greatly underscore the need for innovation.

title: Zero emissions in a circular military economy: from military individual equipment waste to food and energy
objective:
"The ZEROWASTE project uses advanced biotechnologies to produce energy and food for military personnel. The project aims to design systems that can be installed in isolated infrastructure or operational fields, ensuring the disposal of individual military equipment, recovering and reusing CO2 to grow photosynthetic microalgae cells, and producing fresh food and nutraceuticals. Thus, the project also wants to design a modern, sustainable, and clean energy system using wood-based waste materials, transforming wood waste into renewable biofuel (pyrolysis oil) using a thermal process.

The ZEROWASTE project aims to apply natural and renewable biofuel and food technologies for the defence sector, reducing the European Union's dependence on fossil fuels. It focuses on the digestion of military cloths, the production of microalgae, CO2 capturing, and the pyrolysis of wood waste. The project has the potential to be applied to space exploration missions and to new generation of jet-fuel.  

The project focuses on designing sub-systems based on functional requirements, technical setups, and check-lists, ensuring the longevity of components. It aims to contribute to the sustainability and resilience of military operations while reducing environmental impacts and costs. ZEROWASTE is innovative in several ways, including decomposing solid organic materials or synthetic fibers into macromolecules, transforming wood waste into energy, and converting wood waste into storable fuel. This ""win-win"" strategy reduces environmental impacts and costs while promoting the sustainability and resilience of military operations.

All components, including microalgae, filters, pipes, engines, electronics, algorithms, and procedures, will be designed within the EU, with materials, technology, and knowledge coming exclusively from EU resources and industries. The consortium consists of seven participants from six European countries with expertise in environmental, chemical, engineering, and biotech."
keywords: Energy; Food; Oxygen; Jet-fuel; Defence; Innovation; Reuse; Recycling; Individual military equipment

participants.name: CONSORZIO PER L'INNOVAZIONE E LA BIOECONOMIA

title: ARCHitectures based on unconventional accelerators for dependable/energY efficienT AI Systems
objective:
Artificial Intelligence (AI) can power autonomous vehicles, provide strategic advantages through large-scale data analytics, and enable intelligence gathering and surveillance through advanced computer vision, opening a wide range of defence applications.

Conventional Von Neumann architectures, despite their flexibility, are inefficient for AI workloads due to data duplication and data movement bottlenecks, which severely limit the efficiency in the rapid processing of large amount of information and streaming data needed by AI algorithms.

In edge computing devices, crucial for defense applications, this inefficiency is compounded by energy limitations.

To overcome these challenges, specialised hardware and programming paradigm shift are needed to accelerate AI workloads. With the end of Moore’s law and Dennard scaling, simply scaling up existing architectures is no longer viable. Novel architectures are needed to improve performance per Watt and bypass the efficiency limits imposed by the Von Neumann bottleneck.

ARCHYTAS aims to investigate unconventional AI accelerators that take advantage of novel technologies: optoelectronic-based accelerators, volatile and non-volatile processing-in-memory, and neuromorphic devices. These technologies promise to mitigate the Von Neumann bottleneck by integrating processing and memory. ARCHYTAS also explores the integration of CMOS-based systems with analogue accelerators, as well as new programming models to improve the programmability, performance portability, and productivity of these emerging parallel systems through a hardware-AI co-design approach. The technological ambition of ARCHYTAS is to bridge the gaps in multi-modal sensing integration and AI processing, providing solutions that fit the non-functional requirements of future autonomous vehicles for defense applications. 

The ARCHYTAS AI accelerators will be validated within the context of defense AI use cases in land, aerial, maritime, and space settings.

title: Autonomous SSA Bodyguard Onboard Satellite
objective:
BODYGUARD aims to develop building blocks for autonomous SSA capabilities onboard satellites. The included studies will address both use cases of hosted SSA-payload as well dedicated guardian satellites. A sensory system will be designed to detect incoming threats in far and close distances. Onboard processing system will be designed to enable fully autonomous AI-based threat detection, identification, tracking, maneuver recognition and self-protecting action decisions. Countermeasure robotics will be designed to handle physically attacking threat with resilience added to equipment like optics and comms to ensure partial operation under engaged distant threat. This project will complement previous EDF SSA projects by autonomous onboard SSA systems developments, empowered by AI for self-protection needs. BODYGUARD will be able to classify the types of manoeuvres being made from far distance thus rising an alarm early. In close proximity, it can detect weak-points of the threat satellite and counteract with a robot or laser. Detection is accomplished using AI onboard processing from multiple sensor inputs, including highly sensitive optical greyscale and IR cameras with various FOVs, laser ranging and LiDAR systems. Self-protection actions are based on high-intensity laser beam, microwave and high-voltage applications as well as a micro-robot that rendezvous to a weak spot of the threat satellite to disable it. 

The EDF-2023-RA-SPACE-PSA grant allows to perform knowledge generation, studies and design activities. Therefore BODYGUARD will accomplish development-targeted research activities up to TRL4 and TRL5. Technology concept studies through this project will lead to deeper competency in SSA sensors, detection capabilities and protective actions to be taken (e.g. designing autonomous sensors with AI-processing that can be added to GEO-satellite or its In-Orbit-Servicing module and will alarm host satellite about approaching threats).

title: European COmon SupplY chain for Sovereign T2SL and infrarEd Modules.
objective:
Infrared technology is an important element of Europe’s technological sovereignty in key value chains. T2SL detectors are expected to represent a large part of the infrared detectors market in the near future since they can address the entire infrared wavelength range, have an excellent quantum efficiency and array uniformity and operate at high temperatures, allowing for more compactness, lower energy consumption and cost-efficiency. Yet European detector manufacturers who want to propose T2SL products currently need to rely on non-EU industrial sources at least for substrate and epitaxy. 
ECOSYSTEM will consolidate a fully European supply chain for the next generation of high performance T2SL infrared detectors for defence applications. The project will address four main building blocks through: 
1-	Establishment of a European supply chain for GaSb epi ready substrates
2-	Establishment of a European epitaxy supply chain for the main wavelengths for defence applications 
3-	Development of a common post-processing ROIC bumping compatible with the selected advanced CMOS technology node 
4-	Design of improved cryocoolers with SWaP-C optimisation. These results will be demonstrated through prototypes.
Finally, ECOSYSTEM will offer a roadmap to assess the next required steps for strengthening the supply chain. The supply chain will be based on cooperative R&D, will be open to all European detector manufacturers and will target the next generation of high-performance infrared detectors for defence applications in all battlespace dimensions. 
ECOSYSTEM is fully aligned with European priorities as the project will contribute to maximise operational advantages, reinforce European autonomy and support the competitiveness of the European industry.

title: European System for Outsized Cargo Airlift (ESOCA)
objective:
Strategic airlift capabilities are critical to rapidly and effectively deploying large numbers of personnel, heavy equipment, and oversized cargo to meet European and allied nation military ambitions. Historically, strategic airlift capacity has been provided almost entirely by the United States, creating a critical dependency. More recently, strategic airlift was supported by assets of the United Kingdom or carried out by the Antonov Logistics Fleet. However, the Russian attack on Ukraine has devastated the Antonov Logistics fleet. To preserve European sovereignty and enable global force projection it is critical to establish a strategic lift capability in Europe.
The European System for Outsized Cargo Airlift (ESOCA) study shall identify, define, and evaluate short and long-term options for future European strategic airlift capability.

title: next GEneration of IA and combat cloud systems for NeutralIzation of Unexploded threatS
objective:
The standoff detection and neutralization of explosive ordnance (UXO/IED) including radio controlled IED and person and vehicle borne improvised explosive device, is still an important research topic, requiring complex information fusion from various sensors and effectors. 

The solution proposed by GENIUS consortium to the topic EDF-2023-RA-PROTMOB-DEXPLO is based on the experience of other demining and IED detection projects, the knowledge from tactical cloud infrastructures, the lessons learnt from multiple sensor data acquisition and fusion projects. Specifically, is based on the previous experience of three of the winning projects of the EDF-22 Challenge (DIGIT-R-HTP-P /O): CONVOY,  AIDEDex and HITDOC projects, as GENIUS members are part of them.

These three projects will be the starting point of the new GENIUS proposal, where the accuracy of UXO/IED explosive detections, the inclusion of new technologies and new sensors for detection, the inclusion of a new set of effectors, including a UGV for neutralisation and handling, will significantly improve IED and UXO mine counter technologies to deal with new and evolving threats.

GENIUS proposal considers an evaluation campaign in a relevant environment, to demonstrate the capabilities achieved by the developed system, where all the data acquired through their sensors, will be available to enable reproduction of experiments in a simulated environment.

One midcap is coordinating the GENIUS consortium, that includes eighteen (18) partners from seven (7) different Members States. Each partner provides unique and important expertise to support the GENIUS objectives and will facilitate the development of new technologies that each partner cannot achieve easily on its own. 

The consortium is formed by one (1)  midcap, three (3) large industries; five (5) research technology organizations, three (3) academic institutions and six (6) SMEs, two of them CB, in order to contribute to increase the industrial cooperation.
keywords: UXO/IED Intelligent Identification Neutralization Combat Cloud Sensors Platforms Effectors

title: Tactical Advanced Laser Optical Systems: Technologies for High Power Laser, Vulnerability study, Vignette development and Operational Study
objective:
TALOS-TWO will pave the way towards fully European and sovereign 100kW-class laser weapons by 2030. With a focus on the laser source and beam combining modules of the laser effector, the project will contribute to maturing critical LDEW technologies and subsystems and provide a proof of concept that combining technologies can meet the requirements of the end-users in different use cases. 

More specifically, TALOS-TWO will :
- Develop two high-power 1µm combined source demonstrators
- Simulate combining methods using 2µm laser sources (virtual 2µm demonstrator)
- Contribute to establish a European supply chain for 1µm and 2µm laser sources
- Mature technologies needed to develop fully European 2µm (“eye-safe”) laser sources with significant output power increase
- Pioneer dynamic 3D visualisation of the danger zone around the target for a controlled shooting
- Investigate the effects of high-energy lasers on relevant targets to predict the capability of LDEW effectors to defeat these targets 
- Synergise with national programmes for the insertion of TALOS-TWO technologies into national demonstrators
- Provide a roadmap for future developments towards TRL 8 by 2030 for a 1µm 100kW-class LDEW and a 2µm 10kW-class LDEW .

Relying on the results of the PADR project “TALOS” which ended in April 2023, TALOS-TWO brings together 21   European partners from 8 countries. The consortium includes the three main national champions for LDEW in France (CILAS), Italy (Leonardo) and Germany (Rheinmetall), two other large companies, 7 research organisations, 5 SMEs and 1 MidCap, fostering a cross-border cooperation throughout academic and industrial worlds. The LDEW academic and industrial sector will be highly impacted by the TALOS-TWO project outcomes: the project will provide significant potential for economic growth  and innovation in EU defense sector, thus contributing directly to EU excellence and autonomy and putting Europe at the forefront of the LDEW market.

title: Additive Manufacturing of composite based fire resistant materials for stealth, Ballistic Lightweight armored structures’
objective:
The ADMIRABLE project aims to enhance naval vessel capabilities by investing in advanced manufacturing technologies and composite materials. The project seeks to address key challenges, including Size, Weight, Power, and Cost reduction, while improving stealth, fire resistance, transparency to EM emissions and ballistic protection. The focus is on developing multifunctional materials, incorporating electromagnetic and structural functionalities, to improve vessel performance and reduce vulnerability. The project also aims to optimize manufacturing processes, considering factors like flexibility, costs, and time.
Challenges include developing a composite material with multifunctional capabilities through complex design tools based on reliable mathematical models which will be able to predict the behavior of the composite studied. The project also explores alternative advanced manufacturing processes for lightweight structural parts and other critical components on board of naval platforms. Collaboration at the EU level is essential, bringing together expertise in naval engineering, material research, manufacturing methods, electronics, and more. The ADMIRABLE consortium of 11 partners includes major European shipyards, SMEs, universities, and research institutes from five EU countries.
The project's goals extend beyond naval applications, with potential applications in civil, security, and defense sectors. The consortium plans to revolutionize the shipbuilding industry, outperforming current materials, and emphasizes minimizing environmental impact by optimizing energy efficiency, reducing waste, and adopting recycled materials. The overall objective is to contribute to the growth of the European Defense Industry, enhance fleet safety and deterrent power, strengthen EU sovereignty, and address supply-chain issues in the defense sector.

title: Innovative propulsion solutions for land and naval defence applications
objective: CALIPSO addresses great challenges for European defense autonomy, sustainability and operability, focusing on naval and land defense sectors. The project seeks to revolutionize military vehicle propulsion by integrating sustainable fuel technologies (spin-in from civil sector). Key objectives of CALIPSO include: (1) Assessing the feasibility, cost-effectiveness and environmental impact of fossil fuel alternatives for naval and land platforms; (2) Providing roadmaps for the gradual introduction of Innovative Propulsion Systems (IPS) in defense applications; (3) Increasing the competitive advantage of European companies in the defense industry, fostering economic growth & sustainability; (4) Reinforcing EU Defense Technological and Industrial Base autonomy & reducing dependence on non-EU innovations; (5) Fostering cross-border cooperation with a consortium comprising 40 SMEs, high-tech companies, midcaps and research organizations; (6) Enhancing energy supply resilience & reducing EU dependence on imported fossil fuels. The project encompasses Key Innovation Technologies (KITs) and Demonstrations (DEMs) covering the entire value chain, including sustainable fuel storage, refueling, power conversion and usage. CALIPSO strategically aligns with the EU Renewable and Low Carbon Fuels industrial alliance, ensuring fuel flexibility, cutting-edge technologies and market opportunities. Implemented through a structured approach over 36 months, CALIPSO begins with defining operational requirements and concludes with the demonstration of the most promising IPS in a representative military environment. The project aims to achieve significant impacts, including the spin-in of 22 civil R&D projects, involving 14 Member States and Norway, and alignment with EU Green Deal targets. CALIPSO emerges as a key player in enhancing the autonomy of the EU's defense industrial sector, fostering cross-border cooperation and contributing to innovation and sustainability across the entire value chain.

title: Advanced EU-made mineral-based materials for a new generation of ultralight, more resistant, eco-designed, morpho- and REACH-compliant Personal Protection Equipment's hard-components for EU Military
objective:
During the last three decades, Personal Protection Equipment (PPE) has progressively increased in weight by improving its level of protection. Nevertheless, a higher load results in difficulties to manouevre and an increased fatigue for armed personnel on the ground.
IMMUNE aims to address this issue by fully unlocking the potential of EU-made mineral-based advanced materials with promising characteristics in terms of mechanical, thermal and chemical resistance, as well as durability and recyclability. 
IMMUNE partners will implement studies, design, simulation, and tests to select the best way to valorise this EU-sourced, elaborated, produced, and distributed mineral-based material for a first application in a bullet-proof vest Proof-of-Concept. 
The consortium will also pave the way for spinning-off to other PPE components and different applications in various defence sectors (aerospace, ground vehicles, etc.) and therefore contributing to improve the EU defence industry's autonomy and supply chain. 
All the IMMUNE activities will be driven by LCA and LCC in order to improve the final product's recyclability strategy and increase the vest's durability. 
Ultimately, the IMMUNE vest will be 25% lighter, provide better protection performances (reduction by 35% of back face deformation) and 10% cheaper on its life cycle than the current vests on the market.
From textile developers to technical clothing manufacturer materials, by way of eco-design and materials engineering experts, with the support of a consulting firm with a prominent experience in the EU and NATO defence markets, IMMUNE consortium gathers various profiles of 4 EU countries with complementary knowledge, skills and capabilities: one SME (C&V Consulting), two mid-caps (SAERTEX and Paul Boyé), a large company (Segula) and two research centre (RISE, Moratex).

title: Generative Automation of Security Penetration Tests
objective: The convergence of different technologies coming with their own vulnerabilities, such as Internet of Things (IoT), communication networks, Operating Systems (OS) and Artificial Intelligence (AI) systems open-up new attack fronts and shift the attackers’ interest to more sophisticated techniques. As technology advances in cyber defence services, military digital operations, and civilian safety applications along with the complexity of cyber risks, the need for automated cybersecurity assessments becomes increasingly important. The necessity for regular security testing, including penetration testing, has raised awareness of best practices and standards for such assessments. TRITON‘s vision is to overcome defence-specific obstacles associated to the automation of penetration tests, and fully automate the pre- and post- pentesting process adopting Markov chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) decision processes to discover hidden attack paths and the DevSecOps paradigm integrated with well-known pentesting frameworks (e.g., Kali Linux, Aircrack-ng, Metasploit, etc.) focusing on military Security Operation Centres (SOCs), web and heterogeneous cloud applications, telecom and wireless networks. It introduces the novel concept of Human-as-a-Security-Sensor (HaaSS), letting the operators of the automated penetration testing solution to monitor the progress, perform what-if analysis, predict future paths, and enforce controls through security policies. The key idea is to build a wide‐ranging manifold of novel tools and strategies that enable next‐generation ICT systems and networks with distributed devices, to perform automated and Artificial Intelligence (AI) driven security assessments at massive scale. TRITON is expected to realize its vision through generative AI and ethical attacks performed by Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) and proactive risk assessments with optimal mitigation controls targeting code, firmware, networks, and ICT deployment environments.
keywords: Artificial Intelligence; Markov chain Monte Carlo attack paths; generative AI and ethical attacks; Firmware Pentesting; Code Integrity and Verification Pentesting; Actionable User Interfaces

title: 5G Communications for Peacekeeping and Defence 2.0
objective:
5G Communication for Peace Keeping and Defence 2.0 will continue the consolidation and expansion of trusted EU Ecosystem standard for military multi-dimensional operations world-wide using 3GPP evolution. Overall objective is to enabling EDF Member States to procure and evolve coalition capability with complementary civil communication networks and devices. This should increase the European Defence Industry Technology Base, EU independence and security of supply.
The 5G COMPAD project overall reference architecture and use case definition will serve as a foundation of 5G COMPAD 2.0. The 5G COMPAD 2.0 project will bring the following on top of 5G COMPAD:
•	Advanced Capabilities such as network as a sensor, advanced positioning services, automatic network setup, non-terrestrial networks (NTN) and spectrum sharing
•	Enhanced individual systems and integration for full interoperability in a Multi-Domain Operational environment
•	Better Interoperability and integration with existing military infrastructure (existing NATO systems)
•	Standardized security solutions
•	Case-agnostic Minimum Viable Solution for Member States procurement of interoperable, secure, robust and resilient 5G for military use with TRL 7

Six Streams and associated tasks have been defined to address the requirements - Mandatory Studies (MS), Operational Requirements (OR)and Technical Requirements (TR) raised by the different MoDs. 
The capabilities will be demonstrated in several EU member states as well as during CWIX. Six demo events have been planned along the 3-year span of the project. 
The 5G COMPAD 2.0 project consortium consists of leading European 5G technology providers, 5G device providers, defence integrators, specialized SMEs, network operators as well as academia and research institutions.
keywords: 5G Communication Network Peacekeeping Defence 2.0 Ecosystem 3GPP Capability Industry Technology Trusted Procurement Devices Connected Security

title: Artificial Intelligence Warfare Adaptive Swarm Platform
objective: The AI-WASP “Artificial Intelligence Warfare Adaptive Swarm Platform” project aims to develop a pioneering solution of AI-based multifunctional aperture and transceiver. The project targets to develop multifunctional solutions for electromagnetic spectrum operations in future electronic warfare battlefield along with missions for surveillance and establishing tactical mission network. The proposed solution is based on convergence of several existing technologies to a cost-effective multifunctional system, which is enabled by proposed modular open system architecture.  The multifunctionality is accomplished by developing completely new and novel system modules and software components utilizing Artificial Intelligence (AI) throughout the system. AI-WASP project develops a novel modular architecture, with hardware and software reusable modules, where separately developed technologies and functionalities are combined to one system that is designed to work both as an independent system and as networked and multitasking system of systems. The outcome of the project is a prototype of two different system variants that are to be integrated to small and medium size UAS and ground vehicles or ground station. The AI-WASP Consortium is coordinated by Patria and the consortium consists of 16 partners (LEs, SMEs, Mid-Cap and RTO) from nine EU Member States: Finland, Sweden, Estonia, Lithuania, Germany, Greece, France, Spain and Italy.

title: ArmoURed Infantry Ground Assault
objective:
The European Union (EU) aims to achieve greater strategic and industrial autonomy in defence and security sector. The obsolescence and the lack of interoperability of Armoured Infantry Fighting Vehicles (AIFVs) fleets is a significant concern for EU. Under this perspective, the AIFVs fleets are crucial to develop EU defence capabilities and promote military cooperation. Current conflicts have highlighted several AIFVs critical requirements such as protection, mobility, firepower and C4I / Situational Awareness as well as the need for standardization. AURIGA “ArmoURed Infantry Ground Assault” vision is to pave the way to respond to these needs.
AURIGA aims to design, develop and prototype key technology bricks, enabled by an open, scalable and modular system architecture that addresses both current and future threats and requirements. AURIGA system will incorporate innovative and disruptive technologies, and demonstrate the potential for upgrading existing AIFVs where applicable. AURIGA, will be aligned with the Concept of Operations (CONOPS) harmonized across participating EU Member States and Norway.
AURIGA modular and scalable design will offer a strategic and technological leap forward in the AIFV sector, addressing the limitations of ageing and obsolete systems by providing superior protection, enhanced firepower, advanced networking capabilities, increased mobility, future-proof adaptability and interoperability to excel in both conventional and asymmetric warfare while ensuring operational effectiveness and mission success. 
AURIGA will combine cutting-edge performance with cost-effectiveness, achieved through modularity and insertion of civil technology. The technology bricks examined under AURIGA will also pave the way for new applications and spin-offs in both defence and civil sectors.
AURIGA will be implemented through study and design, supported by prototyping, to identify capabilities and technologies in order to pursue project major objectives.

title: Advanced Knowledge for indirect Trajectory Attack at long Range for EU states
objective:
The AktarEUs project addresses the need for greater European defence autonomy and superiority by proposing further enhancements on the Beyond Line Of Sight (BLOS) technology.

Key objectives of AktarEUs are to: (1) Develop an integrated BLOS system in conditions representative of an operational use; (2) Progress in the development of EMS functions; (3) Integrate BLOS functionalities in a battlefield training system; (4) Foster cross-border cooperation, involving 4 SMEs and 1 Mid-Cap; (5) Enhance cost-effectiveness and lifecycle efficiency compared to existing BLOS solutions; (6) Further contribute to the integration of the European defence market with the involvement of 10 entities and support from the PESCO BLOS composed of 4 member states.

AktarEUs will be implemented through twelve work packages.

The project will begin with a feasibility study that will identify the main architectures constraints and mitigate most of the technological risks, followed by a design phase that will allow the creation of 3 main demonstrators (light, medium and heavy) which will be used in a final demonstration including live munition firings.

AktarEUs will be efficiently managed thanks to a concentrated consortium consistent with the development of demonstrator at a scale higher than the previous BLOS projects.

The consortium aims to achieve project objectives within 36 months, with an EDF support of 24,1M€. AktarEUs innovative approach and European collaboration will pave the way to the integration of BLOS systems within operational platforms in the early 2030s.

participants.name: ADDITESS ADVANCED INTEGRATED TECHNOLOGY SOLUTIONS & SERVICES LTD

title: Cyber Infrastructure for Training in Advanced Defence Exercises and Learning
objective:
CITADEL Range seeks to upgrade European defence technology in the field of cyber ranges. The vision is built on the strategic shift in the EU’s cyber defence agenda where a clear move has taken place from cyber responsive operations to full spectrum cyber defence operations. Given this, the next-generation cyber range modular products and concepts aim to provide tailored solutions for the entire value chain of cyber forces. This means that the focus is set strongly on 1) the needs of the EU and its Member States, and 2) the needs of holistic cyber defence as opposed to civilian cyber security. 

The project delivers a selection of advanced capabilities that enable cyber forces to conduct training, exercising, and mission rehearsal in realistic and cost-efficient environments purpose-built for cyber operations - be it national exercises for civil-military cooperation, multi-national exercises for rehearsing common mission execution or EU-wide training events coordinated by the EDA that enhance interoperability and promote seamless information-sharing within the Union. The project shall not deliver a new or standalone cyber range, it delivers technological tools and frameworks that complement already existing cyber ranges in national defence structures. The solutions are modular and thereby any individual result of the project can be used independently of others.

The solutions cover aspects such as cross-border classified exercises; realistic cyber operations toolkits and processes; and integration of cyber within MDO exercises. To better deliver these benefits, the project leverages technologies such as digital twins and AI that enable to create more realistic environments with less resources (time, money, people). All of this is built on top of a common domain-specific language and supported by EU-wide cyber range capability and trainee accreditation frameworks.

title: European Next Generation Rotorcraft Technologies Phase II
objective:
The ENGRT II (EU Next Generation Rotorcraft Technologies) project is a pivotal initiative designed to bolster the European Union's strategic autonomy and ensure its leadership in defense capabilities. By preparing the technological foundations for the next generation of European military rotorcraft, ENGRT II directly contributes to the security of EU citizens and enhances the global competitiveness of the European Defence Technological and Industrial Base (EDITB).
The project aims to deliver cutting-edge rotorcraft technologies ready for integration by 2030, addressing the complex and evolving demands of modern defense.
Building on the successes of its predecessor, ENGRT I, this project pushes the boundaries of innovation across several key domains. It focuses on developing advanced rotorcraft configurations, novel materials, and breakthroughs in aerodynamics to ensure future platforms outperform existing models in speed, range, and operational capability.
A core objective of ENGRT II is to unify the European rotorcraft community around critical strategic topics. The project will establish a common European perspective on a Modular Open System Approach (MOSA), enabling greater interoperability and adaptability for future systems. Furthermore, it will develop key technologies for rotorcraft operating in Multi-Domain Collaborative Combat, a crucial element of modern military doctrine.

 

By fostering intensive cross-border cooperation between industry leaders, research institutions, and 10 specialized SMEs from across the Union, ENGRT II strengthens the EDITB's resilience. This collaborative framework not only reduces reliance on non-EU technology sources but also promotes a more integrated and innovative European defense ecosystem.

The project also emphasizes life-cycle efficiency, integrating sustainable practices to reduce the environmental footprint of rotorcraft operations while maximizing their effectiveness and affordability.

# Military Rotorcraft
# Defense Innovation
# Cross-Border Cooperation"

title: European Protected Waveform (Phase 2)
objective:
The European Protected Waveform (EPW) program is a critical enabler for CSDP operations and missions by both EU member states and EU agencies in providing scalable, secure and resilient communications in peacetime and during operations with protection against intrusion, hacking, jamming, traffic monitoring and eavesdropping. The EPW system will facilitate European autonomy and secure cooperation between EU member states through full interoperability in complex and multi-domain operations over satellite. Synergies between EPW and existing or upcoming EU programs will be maximized to increase the efficiency and cost effectiveness of the EPW program and to integrate EPW within the future EU secure satellite communication ambitions.

The main objective of the multi-year project is to transform the system concepts developed in the first phase into an EPW-capable end-to-end system. The starting points for the second phase are three development tracks identified in the initial work of the EPW program. These focus on advanced satellite communication technologies for point-to-point as well as shared access communications, ensuring compliance with the mandatory system prototype and testing activities. Additional goals include the development of advanced technologies for resilience and end-to-end security.

The EPW consortium brings together a mix of organizations (SMEs, research centers, and large corporations) from across the EU that are experts in the field of satellite communications.
keywords: Satellite Communications Security Protected Waveform Resiliency Space Multi-Domain CSDP IRIS² European

title: European Autonomous Heavy Minesweeping System
objective:
The EU critically depends on keeping shipping lanes open, both for international trade and for military support from allies. Sea mines are cost-effective weapons that can block these shipping lanes. Today, the EU lacks a minesweeping capability to counter mines that are a threat to large commercial ships. EUROSWEEP (European Autonomous Heavy Minesweeping System) will address this shortfall through the study, design, prototyping and demonstration of a common European unmanned minesweeping system with autonomous features that can generate the output required to protect large commercial ships and military re-supply vessels.
The project’s objectives are to: 
•	Give the EU a complementary mine countermeasures capability to be able to operate in different environments and be effective against more threats, such as modern stealth mines
•	Develop a concept of operations and collect Member States' (MS) requirements, to facilitate interoperability
•	Increase seaworthiness, endurance and shock resistance of maritime drones so they can operate longer and further while decreasing their underwater signatures  
•	Push the level of autonomy for maritime drones by integrating onboard sensors and data to plan, execute and adjust military missions without human assistance, understand the environment and operate in radio and/or GNSS-denied situations
•	Develop payloads for alternative missions such as anti-submarine warfare
•	Develop a cybersecurity framework to protect all digital infrastructure, data, information and communication against cyber attacks
•	Foster cross-border cooperation with a consortium comprising 18 partners from 10 MS and Norway, including 10 SMEs
With a total budget of 37M€, implemented through 12 WPs and achieving TRL6, EUROSWEEP will pave the way to the Future European Autonomous Heavy Minesweeping System, supporting EU independence from third countries for critical defence technologies while increasing EU innovation capacity, collaboration and competitiveness.

participants.name: ABEKING & RASMUSSEN SCHIFFS- UND YACHTWERFT SE
Wehrtechnische Dienststelle für Schiffe und Marinewaffen, Maritime Technologie und Forschung

title:
Naval systEm of systems and 
integRatEd sUrvivability of future EU platformS
objective:
NEREUS aims to develop the foundation of the Naval Smart System of Systems (SoS) for future European naval platforms, which should be able to operate interconnected in a fully integrated way under challenging multi-domain (i.e., land, aerial, surface, subsurface, and cyber) threat conditions and under an integral survivability approach.
The main objective of NEREUS is to perform the Conceptual Design of the System of Systems (SoS) for the next generation of European naval platforms. It will identify and define the common operational requirements of the SoS framework and the essential elements, structured in four pillars Combat System, Platform Management System, Communication & Information System and Navigation System, along with a Survivability Advisory System Foundation and Transversal capabilities.
NEREUS proposes a new architecture approach based on a SoS framework using a Service-Oriented-Architecture (SOA). The SoS Framework will integrate the essential elements of the ship in a coherent manner using a common infrastructure, including data and services, and promoting standardization, interoperability and interchangeability. This approach facilitates close coordination among all onboard systems to improve decision-making during operations, thereby maximizing survivability and optimizing the sustainment of Combat System capabilities according to the ship's conditions.
NEREUS will identify the elements to be incorporated into future European naval platforms, considering both current and emerging technologies. A comprehensive roadmap will be developed, identifying technologies and associated elements to be considered and developed for the future European naval platforms, and prioritising these developments for subsequent phases.
NEREUS is a 3.5-year project with a balanced consortium of 24 beneficiaries from 14 European countries, 12 large companies, 4 SMEs, 5 mid-caps, 2 research organisations, 1 naval industrial association,12 affiliated and 25 subcontractors.

title: Technology-Enhanced Military Medical Training for Increased Readiness and Survivability in the Battlefield
objective:
READYMED EUROPE aims to co-create, through a user-centred iterative methodology, a ground-breaking Military Medical Training Federated Platform that will significantly enhance both the preparedness and interoperability among military medical teams, while addressing challenges such as limited access, resource fragmentation, and outdated training methods. It will enable cost-effective and realistic training to improve decision-making, teamwork, and procedural skills critical in high-stakes environments including those involving mass casualties. READYMED seeks to transform military medical training, enhancing the EU's capacity to address critical defence challenges and ensuring the inclusion of personnel in remote locations.

The project will build on the consortium members’ state-of-the-art products for military medical training and the most advanced research outcomes from their major European EDF, EDIDP and Horizon projects in this domain. It will extend and integrate them using state-of-the-art simulation, extended reality, artificial intelligence, and 5G networking technologies to deliver a comprehensive and modular set of medical training modules for Prolonged Field Care, Tactical Combat Casualty Care, CBRNE Training, MASCAL and MEDEVAC Management, as well as Surgical Simulation. An advanced Exercise Control System alongside an AI-driven Learning Management System will the fine grain animation and analysis of joint exercises promoting interoperability in military and civilian medical training.

To lay the groundwork for broad adoption and successful exploitation of the project’s results, the consortium will produce an updated curriculum for prolonged field care that integrates the proposed innovations, develop a final report outlining best practices, recommendations, and a future roadmap for military medical training, and leverage its strong presence in the military medical training and civil protection markets to foster adoption.
keywords: "#Military Medical Simulation and Training #Prolonged Field Care (PFC) # Constructive Simulation #eXtended Reality (XR) #Interoperability #Federated Training Platform # Hybrid Training Solutions #5G #C"

title: Sustainable Energy Capabilities for Enhanced Military Camps and Operations
objective:
SENTINEL addresses the urgent need to shift military energy provision from a high reliance on fossil fuels toward sustainable, resilient, and efficient energy solutions. The project aims to enhance the operational efficiency and tactical autonomy of military camps and operations, while reducing logistic burden and supporting a flexible and high-capacity energy supply. SENTINEL will prototype and qualify in operational conditions with armed forces 17 Key Energy Capabilities encompassing advanced energy technologies, enhanced mobility, digital energy solutions, and smart-grid integration, all tested in realistic military scenarios across various climates.

These capabilities will be demonstrated in five scenarios: (1) autonomous renewable energy systems in temperate and tropical tactical bases, (2) lifecycle demonstration of a hybrid energy base setup, (3) microgrid supporting advanced energy-efficient equipment in arid zones, (4) renewable energy-based operations in temperate and arctic conditions with hybrid and e-vehicles, and (5) a digital platform for designing and operating autonomous energy-efficient camps globally  based on digital twin components, applied for forward operating base of 2000 personnels to tactical operation of 50 personnels.

Built upon the EDF 2021 INDY project, SENTINEL brings together 41 entities, leveraging military and civilian expertise to deliver interoperable, secure, and sustainable energy solutions aligned with all stages of military operations. Key outcomes include enhanced resilience for high and low-intensity scenarios, reduced reliance on fossil fuels, and minimized logistic demands. Additionally, SENTINEL will strengthen the EU’s Defence Technological and Industrial Base by accelerating the military’s green and digital energy transformation, adapting civilian energy technologies for military use, and fostering continuous innovation for Key Energy Capabilities deployment between 2030 and 2050.
keywords: Security and Efficiency; Military Camps and Operations; Mobility; Digital Solutions; Renewable Energy; Interoperability; Smart Micro Grids; Greening Defence

title: SEP-211097550 - Small UAS
objective:
The consortium members would like to make a proposal to this call developing The Small Enhanced European UAS project as an advanced AI-driven swarm system consisting of 10 unmanned aerial systems (UAS), specifically designed for multi-domain operations in air, land, and maritime environments. The project addresses critical operational challenges faced by small UAS, including low signatures, extended operational ranges, autonomy, miniaturization, and survivability in contested and congested airspace.
Swarm Composition:
10 UAS with specialized capabilities:
2 UAS equipped with RF analyzers and jammers for electronic warfare.
2 UAS equipped with miniaturized weapons for precision targeting.
6 UAS with a variety of sensors, day and thermal cameras, and satellite communication systems for intelligence gathering and multi-sensor surveillance.
Payload and Propulsion Capabilities:
Each UAS is designed with a 20 kg payload capacity, allowing the integration of miniaturized weapons, sensors, jammers, and communication systems.
The system uses a combination of hydrogen propulsion for two UAS, ensuring long endurance and environmental sustainability, and hybrid engines with advanced batteries for the remaining eight UAS, optimizing performance and reducing acoustic signatures for stealth operations.
eVTOL (Electric Vertical Take-off and Landing) capability allows for versatile deployment and minimizes logistical constraints, while the detachable wings reduce the UAS's logistical footprint.
The swarm system will be controlled via an integrated C2 center with multi-level access, providing secure communication from a NATO Command Center (with 5 levels of access) directly to the swarm. The system features real-time operational control and the ability for direct interaction between the NATO Command and UAS units.
Quantum encryption will ensure that all communication between command centers, C2 units, and the UAS swarm is secured against cyber threats.
keywords: AI-driven swarm system consisting of 10 unmanned aerial systems (UAS)

participants.name: SSRD, VARNOST PROGRAMSKE OPREME, RAZISKAVE IN RAZVOJ, D.O.O.

ADDITESS ADVANCED INTEGRATED TECHNOLOGY SOLUTIONS & SERVICES LTD

title: Vertical Autonomous Next-generation Tactical Aerial European drone
objective:
Drone operations have added a new chapter to modern warfare. Uncrewed systems are disrupting the way modern warfare is being fought, and rapid technological adaptation and continuous innovation in UAS will be critical in future warfare, therefore, also for the European security interests.

Therefore, VANTAGE project aims to develop the next generation of European tactical drones, improving operational efficiency and integrating cutting-edge C4ISR systems. The project’s ambition is to address key operational challenges such as limited operational range, vulnerability to detection, and the need for real-time data sharing. 

The project will develop a flexible and modular long-range VTOL platform that can perform various including C4ISR and kinetic strikes. This platform will be designed for adaptability, making it easy to integrate new sensors and navigation systems. This ensures that the drone can operate effectively in diverse environments, even in conditions where GPS/GNSS signals are denied or unavailable.

Considering the European needs, Ukrainian example and current technological state-of-the-art in the drone industry, VANTAGE expects to develop several disruptive and innovative technologies that will significantly enhance UAS performance in complex and contested environments: (1) GNSS-Denied and Electronic Warfare resilient Navigation; (2) Swarm and Manned-Unmanned Teaming; (3) Autonomous Flight Control and Dynamic Re-Planning and Integrated Multi-Operator Control; (4) 5G datalink.

Project consortium consists of 8 different entities from 6 EU countries. VANTAGE will increase cross-border partnerships within EU, involving SMEs, RTOs and Large Enterprises, for combining cutting-edge expertise and innovation capabilities.

participants.name: "INSTITUTUL NATIONAL DE CERCETARE-DEZVOLTARE AEROSPATIALA ""ELIE CARAFOLI""- INCAS BUCURESTI"
CENTRO DE OBSERVACION Y TELEDETECCION ESPACIAL SAU

title: Integrated Modular Unmanned Ground System 2
objective:
iMUGS2 aims to advance the innovative technologies developed under the previous iMUGS project, focusing on unmanned ground systems (UGS), with higher levels of autonomy and interoperability for European defence forces.
The iMUGS2 project aims to develop an unmanned ground system capable of manned-unmanned and unmanned-unmanned teaming with other robotic unmanned platforms and manned infantry vehicles to provide them full battlefield support. The proposed solution will feature a modular, multi-agent system architecture, incorporating autonomy and swarming technologies, among others. 
The iMUGS2 solution aims to be free from any non-EU export control restrictions, balances performance and cost-effectiveness, reducing ownership costs through high maintainability and improved life cycle efficiency, while offering flexible, scalable procurement options that accelerate and future-proof capability acquisitions for European armed forces.
iMUGS2 will create an open ecosystem and large-scale European collaboration that will pave the way to enhanced unmanned capabilities ready for procurement and integration into EU forces by 2030. This will increase the competitive advantage of European companies in the worldwide UGS industry, fostering economic growth and high-tech job creation.

title: First development action for Medical Counter Measures performed in the frame of the RESILIENCE FPA consortium
objective:
The European Union (EU) faces growing security challenges due to international instability, exemplified by the war in Ukraine and the COVID-19 pandemic. Among these challenges are rising Chemical, Biological, Radiological, and Nuclear (CBRN) threats. To enhance resilience, it is crucial for Europe to develop medical countermeasures (MCMs) to protect both military and civilian populations. To address this gap, the RESILIENCE Framework Partnership Agreement (FPA) was established in 2022, providing a multi-annual action plan aiming to strengthen European MCM research and development against CBRN threats, based on three pillars: i) Diagnosis, ii) Prevention & iii) Prophylaxis and Treatment.

The RESILIENCE-D-2024 project is the first development action under this plan, comprising two sub-projects: Operational dosimetric evaluation of soldiers health (DOSI), and Advanced solutions to foster the design and production of proteins for MCM (PROT PROD), efficiently coordinated by a transversal management, Concept Development and Experimentation action. DOSI focuses on developing an integrated dosimetry modular architecture for the personalised medical management of soldiers exposed to NR treats, based on the individual dose reconstruction. PROT PROD focus on the development and technology assessment of alternative manufacturing platforms for protein-based MCMs in a scalable and Good Manufacturing Practices-compatible format, improving production speed, scalability, and cost-efficiency while reducing dependency on traditional methods.

The project involves collaborations among 11 organisations from five European countries, including military, academic and industrial partners. 

The ultimate goal is to advance the development of innovative dosimetry systems and alternative protein production tools to address unmet needs in CBRN defence, thereby enhancing Europe's security, strategic EDTIB autonomy and security of supply.

coordinator.name: COMMISSARIAT A L ENERGIE ATOMIQUE ET AUX ENERGIES ALTERNATIVES

participants.name: COMMISSARIAT A L ENERGIE ATOMIQUE ET AUX ENERGIES ALTERNATIVES
AIT AUSTRIAN INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY GMBH
AUTORITE DE SURETE NUCLEAIRE ET DE RADIOPROTECTION

title: Autonomous QUick Insertion and Launch of AUVs
objective: The increasing strategic importance of the seabed warfare has positioned this domain as a critical arena in modern military strategy. In a context marked by a trend toward increased use of drones, there is a growing need for projecting capabilities over long distances, using lightweight, autonomous, and interoperable systems. Micro-Autonomous Underwater Vehicles (AUVs), play a crucial role to tackle these challenges. AQUILA will develop a new approach for seabed control in Anti-Submarine Warfare and Rapid Environmental Assessment relying on a system uniquely combining heterogeneous drones (i.e. micro-AUV and UAV) and vessels to enable the execution of operation, including communication of mission data, within a 100 km range. AQUILA will develop, integrate and test technological solutions required to achieve an innovative, autonomous, easy-to-deploy and cost-effective end-to-end system that will enable i) transportation and launch of up to 4 micro-AUVs from an aerial drone (UAV); ii) airdrop deployment from a maximum altitude of 2 km without damage to the micro-AUV; iii) surface-to-air communication within a 3 km radius; iv) mission planning and data recovery and v) surface recovery of the micro-AUV from a UAV. AQUILA gathers 4 SMEs from 4 Member States. The outcome of the project will be the development of a modular system that includes: a launching platform, a communication system, a robust airdrop-resistant and air-transportable micro-AUV supported by a recovery deployable system. AQUILA will enhance the deployability of AUV for naval operations. AQUILA will be a game changer by establishing key expertise in air-launch capabilities within Europe and positioning the EU to compete head-to-head with other nations in the AUV sector. The project will also strengthen the European Union's strategic autonomy and sovereignty by developing a fully European solution for military operations and reconnaissance in marine environments.

participants.name: MARESCO MONOPROSOPI ETAIREIA PERIORISMENIS EFTHYNIS EMPORIAS & ANTIPROSOPEIAS EPISTIMONIKON ORGANON

title: Camofoil 2.0 - Advanced material for disruptive approach to signature management
objective:
Current camouflaging and heat signature management systems are static and expensive with limited compliance to dynamic warfare
and limited technological scalability potential. These solutions are not on-field adaptable, and the enabling technology does not
accommodate rapid reengineering and field reapplication to comply with high pace technology-turnover-rate within sensor systems
and their AI assisted pattern recognition software.

In this project the consortium intent to develop, test and demonstrate a platform agnostic, visual, thermal and radar camouflaging solution: a new more dynamic approach to camouflaging. A disposable foil for multispectral signature management applicable for
armored vehicles and beyond, based on an enabling technology that renders it upgradeable to keep up with technology
advancement speeds and remain unrecognized by emerging sensor threats and AIs.

Camofoil 2.0 is a disposable signature management system, protecting against recognition across the electromagnetic spectrum
from UV to Radar. The foil can be supplied in any colour or camouflage pattern. It may be applied as stickers or tape and cut into
shape to fit any surface or object and installed, repaired and replaced on-site and on demand, as a stand-alone or supplementing
solution, for any platform and across domain, and, based on the technological scalability potential and low-cost, with an inherent
ease of upgrade.

The proposed action will significantly contribute to the competitiveness of the partners and strengthen the European Defence
Technological and Industrial Base autonomy, security of supply and reduce The European Union’s Third-country dependency.
The proposed solution is well in line with EUs Capability Priorities in being an advanced material for both ground combat systems,
but not restricted to this; cross-domain applicable supporting harmonization of capabilities; is modular and updatable as a dynamic,
disruptive and futureproof approach to signature man.
