title: GPT-Powered Common Operational Picture with Autonomous UxV Swarms
objective:
The COP-GPT project aims to develop an AI-driven, real-time Common Operational Picture (COP) for modern military operations, integrating multi-domain autonomous Unmanned Systems (UxS) swarms capable of independent deployment and resilient to electronic warfare. COP-GPT leverages AI, machine vision, Large Language Models (LLMs) and advanced UxS platforms to enable seamless human-machine teaming (HMT), allowing operators to issue high-level commands through natural language, reducing cognitive load and training requirements.

COP-GPT addresses critical operational challenges in contested environments, such as GNSS-independent navigation, resilience to jamming and spoofing, and processing large volumes of heterogeneous data in real-time. The system combines multi-domain UxS swarming with 3D semantic mapping to create an integrated operational picture, giving users accurate situational awareness and decision-making support in GNSS-denied or communications-limited (DDIL) conditions. Key innovations include a self-deploying, autonomous Launch and Recovery System (LRS) for unmanned aerial systems, resilient communication solutions, and edge computing for autonomous data fusion at the tactical edge.

Led by a consortium of European SMEs, COP-GPT enhances European defence autonomy by reducing dependence on non-EU technologies, while also promoting interoperability and modularity. This solution offers strategic advantages by streamlining data integration across domains, reducing operator workloads, and enabling adaptive, autonomous mission planning. COP-GPT’s capabilities provide a significant leap in situational awareness, decision speed, and operational resilience, setting a new standard in autonomous defence systems for the tactical battlefield.

title: Harmonised Approach for Resilient Proactive Intelligence Assessment in DEFENCE
objective: "HARPIA-Defence represents an advancement in defence technology that integrates AI-driven surveillance, secure communications, and autonomous systems into a unified defence ecosystem. HARPIA-Defence's ecosystem is built upon nine interconnected pillars that work in perfect synchronization, with the HERMES-Hub serving as the central cybersecurity intelligence centre for collecting and analysing security data, while the HUS-Net provides comprehensive surveillance through drones and ground vehicles. The system's operational control is managed through COpCon-Face, which serves as the central interface, working alongside BlockSe-Com for encrypted blockchain-enabled communications and SA-Con for secure access control. It is protected by AUTOCYBER-Sol, which acts as an automated cyber threat detection and response system, while DYNAMO-Mech optimizes network management, and AIPAY-Ops enables autonomous operations. The QUALIFORCE-Comp ensures all components meet military standards, creating a comprehensive and robust defence system that maintains continuous communication and support between all pillars. The system's key innovations lie in its ""All-in-One solution"" approach, which uniquely combines cybersecurity, surveillance, and communications in a single ecosystem. HARPIA-Defence's value proposition is multifaceted, offering enhanced situational awareness through continuous high-resolution surveillance, improved cybersecurity through AI and blockchain technologies, and optimized resource management that reduces operational costs. The system's ability to operate in GPS-denied environments, its blockchain-enabled secure communications, and its self-healing network capabilities set it apart from traditional defence systems.  HARPIA-Defence's modular architecture ensures adaptability to evolving threats while maintaining continuous operational effectiveness, making it a significant advancement for European defence capabilities and allied force coordination."

title: Live Operational Data Enhancement for Situational Awareness Through Augmented Reality, part II
objective:
LODESTAR II deeply integrates new enabling technologies from the fields of Augmented Reality (AR) and Artificial Intelligence (AI) within the soldier system. These new technologies are applied with the purpose of increasing the survivability and effectiveness of the (dismounted) soldier. AI and AR as enabling technologies have already been demonstrated convincingly within previous EDF Research project LODESTAR (grant agreement 101102526); the feasibility of key concepts presented in this proposal have previously been proven. It has been demonstrated how we can leverage and combine these emerging technologies within the soldier system, taking into technical limitations in terms of devices’ size, weight, power-energy sources, and computational processing power as well as the limits of human performance, such as logistic burden impact and limits of soldier’s cognitive capacity. An tentative architecture for integrated soldier systems prepared for integration of AI and AR was established and demonstrated in LODESTAR I. 

LODESTAR II will integrate findings from LODESTAR I with the latest insights, implement prototypes based on the LODESTAR architecture, which are then tested and evaluated under representative (simulated) operational conditions. The work done in LODESTAR II will be focussed on building the ability and know-how within the consortium to bring future products to market (downstream of the proposed project) in the shortest possible timeframe. 

The prototype system will include novel hardware and software components (at TRL7) covering every zones of the dismounted soldier system, including a head-mounted AR system, a body-worn AI processor (vision computer) and a highly advanced AR weapon sight. The LODESTAR architecture is fully optimised for through-lifecycle maintanability and upgradeability.

participants.name: E&Q ENGINEERING SOLUTIONS AND INNOVATION SL

title: Space Integrated system for GNSS Monitoring and Analysis
Smart Vehicle Dynamic Controller
objective: The SVDC project will develop a Smart Vehicle Dynamic Controller, an innovative and European solution for critical control systems in defence applications. The SVDC will be capable of monitoring and autonomously adjusting various vehicle subsystems to achieve optimal vehicle performance under changing conditions. Utilizing AI algorithms, it will  autonomously adjust parameters according to situation and mission, thereby reducing crew workload and enabling them to concentrate on mission objectives.

participants.name: ADDITESS ADVANCED INTEGRATED TECHNOLOGY SOLUTIONS & SERVICES LTD

title: Veracity of Earth Observation Data for Defense Applications
objective:
In modern warfare, accurate and reliable data are of paramount importance. The satellite and airborne imagery is extensively used by various services of armed forces for strategic, tactical and operational purposes. Advanced cyber-related vectors of attack from hostile parties exist, which have for objective manipulation and forgery of Earth Observation data in order to present falsified information at various levels of military decision-making. 

Veracity of such data is therefore of critical nature for armed forces, especially when data streams from multiple sensors are used and combined to achieve a multi-layered data analysis approach. This is, indeed, the case for Earth Observation data coming from multiple satellite sources (including in various spectra) which can be of both military and civil provenance as well as airborne drone systems performing observation / reconnaissance missions. 

In this context, the VEODDA project will develop a prototype-stage demonstrator of a cybersecurity solution based on the technology of blockchain to guarantee the veracity of Earth Observation data from an end-to-end perspective. 
This will be done for a combination of data sources coming both from satellite and airborne sensors, throughout their respective processing chains on-board, within the ground segment and up to the analysis and display of the data to the final user who will be able to verify the authenticity of the resulting data. 
The VEODDA project involves phases of feasibility study including analysis of end-user needs, deriving of related system requirements, followed by system design and development activities, building of the prototype and its testing in relevant environment to achieve the target TRL 7 level. Moreover, the VEODDA project will demonstrate the capability of using this system for secured training of AI models for defence scenarios in order to guarantee integrity of AI models for defence applications. It will be a game-changer.

title: AI Reconnaissance for Geo-based Operational Systems
objective:
“ARGOS is a European collaborative project funded by the European Defence Fund (EDF) with the objective of advancing artificial intelligence (AI) technologies for automated satellite image analysis. The project aims to enhance Europe’s strategic autonomy by developing AI-driven solutions that support the production of imagery intelligence for defence applications.
Through a series of challenge campaigns, ARGOS will design, train, and validate a range of AI models tailored to specific use cases. These models will automatically detect, classify, and monitor features in satellite imagery—integrating both optical and radar data—to support more accurate and timely decision-making in defence operations.
The technical approach combines high-performance model development with secure deployment in containerized demonstrators. These demonstrators will be tested and delivered in response to simulated operational needs, ensuring readiness for real-world defence environments. ARGOS also includes complementary outputs such as scientific publications and a roadmap for post-project exploitation.
The consortium brings together leading European organisations in Earth Observation and Security, enabling broad technological coverage and flexible team formation. By using EU-developed technologies and fostering cross-border cooperation, ARGOS will contribute to strengthening the European Defence Technological and Industrial Base (EDTIB) and support Europe’s operational and technological independence.”

participants.name: PLANETEK HELLAS MONOPROSOPI ETAIREIA PERIORISMENIS EFTHYNIS
UNIVERSITA DEGLI STUDI DI NAPOLI FEDERICO II
INSTITUTO NACIONAL DE TECNICA AEROESPACIAL ESTEBAN TERRADAS

title: Advanced Reconnaissance and Geospatial Unified Surveillance
objective:
ARGUS will develop next-generation satellite imagery analysis capabilities for European security through innovative multi-source data fusion combining optical and Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) satellite data. The project will leverage cutting-edge artificial intelligence, high-performance computing, and will explore quantum machine learning techniques to deliver unprecedented speed and accuracy in public and private asset detection and recognition for improved near real-time situational awareness.
The project will address critical challenges in IMINT (Imagery Intelligence) and GEOINT (Geospatial Intelligence) by enabling all-weather, day-night monitoring capabilities that aim to reduce satellite imagery analysis time from days to hours, a two-order-of-magnitude improvement. Robust and resilient advanced AI models will perform semantic segmentation, object detection, change detection, and characteristic estimation across high and very-high resolution imagery.
ARGUS ensures EU strategic autonomy through an entirely EU-based development approach involving seven organizations from six member states, with six specialized SMEs leading technical innovation. The scalable, cloud-native architecture will support deployment from tactical edge computing to strategic command centers, while quantum-ready algorithms will position the system for future technological advances.
The project will deliver significant outcomes across multiple domains. Validated AI models will demonstrate superior performance in annual evaluation campaigns, establishing new benchmarks for satellite imagery intelligence analysis. User-friendly demonstrators will seamlessly integrate with existing systems, enabling rapid operational deployment. Enhanced cross-border cooperation will strengthen the European Defence Technological and Industrial Base (EDTIB) while reducing dependency on non-EU technologies for critical capabilities. ARGUS technologies will also provide capabilities useful for monitoring critical infrastructure and assets and supporting disaster response operations.
ARGUS aims to establish Europe as a global leader in AI-powered satellite imagery analysis, transforming intelligence operations from time-intensive manual processes to AI-assisted real-time insights that will give EU decisive operational advantages and strengthen European strategic autonomy in an increasingly complex security landscape.

participants.name: HI IBERIA INGENIERIA Y PROYECTOS SL
RISE RESEARCH INSTITUTES OF SWEDEN AB

title: Multi-source satellite imagery ARTificial INtelligence Analysis challenge
objective:
The increasing amounts of satellite data, combined with the increased number of areas of interest to monitor or retrieve fresh intelligence from, pushes the need for reliable automation of the analysis and reporting process. Artificial Intelligence (AI), Machine Learning (ML) and Large Language Model (LLM) techniques are rapidly developing, also in the satellite imagery analysis process tasks but objective evaluation of the performance of these solutions is currently not feasible.

To address this problem MARTINA organises a 4 year technical challenge.  The total duration of the challenge will be four years with one campaign per year. It will provide a framework and extensive reference data set to assess and evaluate the accuracy and reliability of AI/ML based automated satellite imagery analysis solutions for different defence use cases offered by three participating consortia. There is an additional focus on combining multiple image data types with different characteristics like, electro optical and radar images, to maximise information extraction. MARTINA will develop the required infra structure and software platform, develop clear guidelines and procedures to establish the transparent and realistic datasets for defence specific use cases, collect and label/annotate the required imagery for the tests, establish clear and objective evaluation methods and perform the actual tests and evaluations.

MARTINA will engage with EU stakeholders in order to provide representative use cases of interest for defence, and to contribute to collaboration and knowledge sharing at the EU level. The stakeholders will also be involved in the definition of the (annotated) datasets. The emphasis of the evaluation will be on the performance in terms of reliable correct detection, classification and characterization and less on the performance in throughput  and speed. Computing requirements or scalability of the solutions is not specifically addressed in this challenge but solution suppliers will have to address these topics in commercial applications. Each campaign exists of the selection of the use cases, data requirement definition (shared with challenge participants), data collection, data annotation and labelling, sharing of training set to the participants, the actual test on the test data in a limited time period and in a controlled manner and the evaluation of the results from each of the participants. The first campaign will be a test run to check and fine tune the procedures, data formats and workflows between the organising consortium and the participating consortia to provide an agreed testing environment and procedures. The other three campaigns will focus on specific use cases with increasing complexity and integration of advanced reporting aspects.

The User Board is a crucial element of MARTINA, consisting of representatives from various European Ministries of Defense (MoDs) and other relevant EU entities. Its responsibilities include providing strategic guidance during each campaign, ensuring that use cases and locations are relevant to real-world defense scenarios, and contributing to the evaluation criteria for the AI applications developed by the participants. They will also test the demonstrators built by the participants in a qualitative manner, with aim to provide the possibility to test it with their own data in their own secure environment. The involvement is expected to require a commitment of approximately 5-10 days per year. The active engagement of the user board is essential for delivering high-impact, actionable solutions for European defense operations.

title: Multi-source information sYstem based on Remote sensing aI Analytics to support the strategic Defence domain
objective:
The MYRIAD (Mutli-source information sYstem based on Remote sensing aI Analytics to support the strategic Defence domain) proposal is based on leveraging latest technological advancements and previous research conducted in the field to enhance their performance and provide them with the capability to generalize the detection, segmentation, and thematic characterization of a large list of targets within complex and broad scenes in the defence context. For that purpose, the complementary nature of optical and radar imagery is enforced by merging different information channels into the same analytic context. Other sensors would be explored as well but provided that their availability is guaranteed. 
MYRIAD will develop an end-to-end solution that capable of deploying all the analytic, interfacing, and logistic modules into the infrastructure of the challenge organizer. There, the dockerized version will be automatically executed to run the use cases that are defined, and the results will be used to generate a set of metrics that will quantify the performance of the solution. Additionally, a standalone version with an independent interface will be developed to allow users and stakeholders to interact with the solution in blind mode by using their own facilities. This version will be designed to operate independently of external cloud resources and will be capable of ingesting any data that users would have available at their own. 
MYRIAD pursues the goal to continuously upgrade the proposed solutions through four annual innovation cycles. Each cycle will incorporate ongoing improvements via an agile development strategy, driven by intra-cycle enhancements. The feedback of users and stakeholders is paramount to nurture the enhancements as well as adding new potential features and capabilities. 

Therefore,MYRIAD will have two main parallel activities: i) the design and implementation of the analytic modules, and ii) the development of the technological framework.

participants.name: NEDERLANDSE ORGANISATIE VOOR TOEGEPAST NATUURWETENSCHAPPELIJK ONDERZOEK TNO
AIT AUSTRIAN INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY GMBH

title: NAVISENSE-X: Autonomous UAV Navigation in GNSS-Denied Environments through Multi-Sensor Fusion and AI-Driven Algorithms
objective:
"NAVISENSE-X is a flagship European defence research initiative aimed at developing a new generation of autonomous drone swarms capable of operating in environments where satellite-based navigation (like GPS) is unavailable or disrupted. it is is a Participant Project in a Technological Challenge, under the frame of an organizing challenge project. 

At the core of NAVISENSE-X is its pioneering use of neuromorphic processing and hyperdimensional computing, advanced approaches that enable seamless fusion of multi-modal sensor data to support autonomous navigation in drone-in-a-swarm formation/capacities. This sophisticated sensor architecture is optimized for resilience and adaptability, ensuring precise navigation and robust situational awareness in GNSS-denied environments, a necessity for Defence operations in areas where traditional navigation systems are compromised.

NAVISENSE-X goes beyond single-UAS autonomy by introducing a framework for cohesive swarm intelligence, which leverages distributed AI agents across a network of drones, empowering them to self-organize, share critical information and adapt dynamically to mission changes or hostile interference. Through Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) and spiking neural networks, the swarm’s architecture is designed to operate as a unified, resilient force that can withstand the loss of individual units and function effectively even under electronic warfare conditions. The neuromorphic sensor processing system, enhances energy efficiency and processing speed, enabling real-time responsiveness to environmental changes. This innovation not only advances the mandate for autonomous, low-SWaP-C (Size, Weight, Power and Cost) UAS platforms but also positions NAVISENSE-X as a transformative solution for high-stakes Defence missions, where operational resilience and real-time adaptability are paramount.

Additionally, NAVISENSE-X introduces a flexible payload-sharing capability within the swarm, enabling heterogeneous drones to carry varied sensor and mission-specific payloads, thereby extending the operational scope and versatility of the swarm. This adaptability allows each drone to contribute unique capabilities—whether for reconnaissance, communications, or electronic countermeasures—maximizing mission effectiveness by leveraging a diversified array of tools and sensors across the swarm. This self-organizing, self-healing architecture not only aligns with the European Defence Priorities emphasising on resilience and adaptability in non-permissive environments but also establishes NAVISENSE-X as a groundbreaking solution for high-stakes Defence missions, where sustained autonomy, operational resilience and real-time adaptability are crucial. To reiterate on the above, NAVISENSE-X advances the advanced/high notion of a ""Multi-Role Autonomous Drone Swarm System""; via the integration of adaptive AI-driven swarm intelligence, real-time neuromorphic processing and multi-payload cap.

Designed and developed entirely within the EU, NAVISENSE-X supports Europe’s goal of strategic defence autonomy. It contributes to a resilient, scalable, and energy-efficient platform that reinforces the EU’s leadership in future battlefield technologies—where adaptability, cooperation, and autonomy are key.
ability to deliver resilient, versatile operations across diverse Defence missions in GNSS-denied, contested environments."

title: Operational Resilience of Drones in Experiments and Autonomous fLight
objective:
The ORDEAL project will host organise a technological challenge environment and test procedure for participating projects participants to demonstrate accurate positioning in non-permissive environments for single and swarm drones.

The EC has chosen, as part of the EDF-2024-LS-RA-CHALLENGE-SENS-RADNP call, complementary to ORDEAL, four participant consortia. The participants projects named themselves, in alphabetic order, NAVISENSE-X, ORIGAMI, SNADE and SWARMER. Details can be found in their project action related documentation.

Guidelines will be provided to challenge participants projects on how to effectively use the data management and sharing platform to work together and share collected sensor data. These guidelines will be documented in the Collaboration Agreement, to be signed by both ORDEAL and all participating projects.

After each field test (except for the last year), a data-based test will take place, where data will be shared among participating projects. This allows participants them to refine their positioning algorithms and gain new insights.
A secure platform will be used to collect annotated sensor data, provided to the challenge organizer, ORDEAL, and made available to other participating projects? teams in the context of the challenge. The platform prioritizes security without compromising efficiency, collaboration, and productivity.

The ORDEAL project is structured into three phases:
•	Phase 1: Challenge Preparation This phase focuses on engaging with stakeholders like the involved MoD’s to define the challenge for the year and preparing for the four field-test campaigns. The outcome of this phase is a detailed test plan and procedure that is shared with challenge participants to help them prepare for the field test and data-based test.
•	Phase 2: Field Test and Data-Based Test This phase involves conducting the field test and data-based test, providing challenge conditions for participants. The phase concludes with test reports on both tests, which serve as input for the evaluation phase.
•	Phase 3: Evaluation In this final phase, all results and findings from participants are evaluated using developed scoring and measurement methods. Participants are compared to each other, and evaluation results are used to track progress from one test campaign to the next. The phase concludes with a debrief workshop where feedback is collected from end-users and participating projects to inform improvements for future test campaigns.

The ORDEAL project's technologies and knowledge have broad applications across various defence capabilities, including enhancing situational awareness, autonomous operation, and secure communications for ground, marine, and air vehicles. The advancements can be applied to intelligence, surveillance, reconnaissance missions, counter-UAV systems, disaster response, training simulations, and logistics operations. The project's outcomes can also be used beyond drone-related solutions in the defence and security sector, with potential applications in unmanned systems on land, sea, and space. The project's focus on test planning, evaluation, and UAV jamming can strengthen defence capabilities by building expertise, fostering innovation, and establishing testing frameworks. Theis technological challenge organized by ORDEAL will encourage collaboration among stakeholders, sparking innovation and advancing practical solutions that can apply to other defence domains.

The ORDEAL consortium brings together partners with diverse expertise, resources, and technical capabilities to tackle the technological challenge. The consortium's composition is carefully designed to meet the specific needs of the project, ensuring that fundamental knowledge is translated into practical solutions for EU Member States armed forces.

title: DrOne swaRm navIgation in Gnss-degraded And non-perMissive envIronments
objective:
The ORIGAMI project is designed to enhance European defence capabilities through the development of autonomous GNSS-free navigation systems for drone swarms in challenging environments. Addressing the evolving security landscape, ORIGAMI presents an innovative solution to overcome vulnerabilities related to RF and GNSS dependencies. 

Drones have become integral to modern warfare, enhancing situational awareness and operational effectiveness. However, reliance on traditional navigation methods can compromise mission success in high-intensity warfare, as evidenced by the Russian jamming tactics in Ukraine. To address these vulnerabilities, ORIGAMI will mature existing technologies and methodologies through an iterative testing approach. This includes a multi-sensor integration strategy, incorporating (initially) nine advanced sensing technologies, such as a novel ultra-low latency laser-scanning sensor for real-time spatial awareness. This approach, combined with robust autonomous navigation capabilities, will enable drones to effectively operate in non-permissive environments.

The ORIGAMI system prioritises low-latency, secure communication networks, ensuring reliable data exchange even amidst electronic warfare threats. The use of fault-tolerant multi-agent swarming enhances resilience and adaptability, allowing the drone swarm to operate cohesively, even under adverse conditions. Additionally, a focus on low-SWaPC design principles ensures that the drones remain lightweight and energy-efficient, facilitating large-scale deployment.

The ORIGAMI consortium, consisting of SMEs, academic institutions, and military organisations, fosters innovation through close collaboration. By leveraging open architectures, the project strengthens EU strategic autonomy in defence technologies, promoting interoperability within the EDTIB. ORIGAMI's iterative approach ensures that the developed drone technologies are robust, reliable, and ready for real-world military applications.
keywords: drones; unmanned aircraft systems; swarming; resilience; GNSS-denied

title: Smart Navigation for Autonomous Drones in Extreme Environments
objective:
Smart Navigation for Autonomous Drones in Extreme Environments (SNADE)

To fulfill their missions, the next generation of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAS) are expected to offer an increased level of autonomy. Their effective deployment requires robust swarming and navigation capabilities when Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) availability is contested or denied and, more generally, in non-permissive environments. The use of various types of sensors (e.g., inertial, optical, infrared, hyperspectral, radar, LIDAR, acoustic, etc.) and intelligent information fusion is necessary to provide the capabilities required to tackle these technical and operational challenges. Such intelligent navigation payloads should be usable on a wide range of unmanned assets, including swarm formations, while ensuring a low SWaP-C (Size, Weight, Power and Costs). Moreover, their performances should be measured in a quantitative, objective, and comparable way. New technological approaches to overcome these barriers are developed in the EDF research action challenge project SNADE.

The unmanned aerial vehicles and systems should be able to accurately estimate their positions and to reach designated target areas with high reliability in non-permissive environments subject to GNSS and sensor denial. The performance of these capabilities will be assessed through test campaigns conducted within the framework of the technological challenge organized by ORDEAL. Specifically, the UASs will be able to record the data acquired through their sensors to enable full replay of flights and reproduction of experiments in a software environment. The proposed approaches represent a cornerstone for future integration and operational missions, especially in terms of SWaP-C.

Our goal is to bring these requirements to life through a collaborative, multi-disciplinary consortium, comprising universities and companies from across Europe, each committed to their specialized roles.

title: Swarming Autonomy in non-peRMissive EnviRonments
objective:
The SWARMER project aims to develop a new generation of autonomous unmanned aerial systems (UAS) designed to operate reliably in environments where satellite-based navigation systems like GNSS are unavailable or actively disrupted. As modern conflicts increasingly involve electronic warfare tactics such as jamming and spoofing, there is a critical need for drones that can navigate, coordinate, and complete missions without relying on GNSS signals.

To meet this challenge, SWARMER will create a swarm of drones capable of robust, autonomous navigation through the integration of multiple onboard sensors and advanced data fusion technologies. These systems will enable drones to perform tasks such as reconnaissance and collaborative operations, adapting in real time to changing conditions on the ground.

The project aligns with several of the European Union’s 2023 Capability Development Priorities, including Ground Combat, Military Mobility, Infrastructure Protection, and Airborne Command and Control. It is supported by the Ministries of Defence of Greece, Poland, Cyprus, and Ireland, reflecting strong operational interest and commitment to the project’s goals.

SWARMER is part of the EDF-2024-LS-RA-CHALLENGE-SENS-RADNP call: “Multi-sensor integration for robust autonomous drone navigation – Participation in a technological challenge.”

ETHNIKO KENTRO EREVNAS KAI TECHNOLOGIKIS ANAPTYXIS

title: Disruptive structural radar ABsorBing and EMI shielding cOmposite Technologies
objective:
The ABBOT project pioneers advanced radar-absorbing (RAM) and electromagnetic interference (EMI) shielding composites to meet the critical needs of modern defense systems. As warfare increasingly relies on electronic systems, ABBOT addresses the urgent demand for materials that enhance stealth and protect equipment from disruptive EM threats. The project’s breakthrough lies in embedding RAM and EMI shielding properties directly into lightweight, durable composite structures, marking a departure from traditional techniques like coatings. ABBOT leverages bio-based vitrimers, engineered thermoplastics, and MXenes—2D nanomaterials with exceptional microwave properties—to develop sustainable composites that align with EU goals for environmental responsibility.

ABBOT introduces an innovative multi-tiered approach, integrating material synthesis, advanced manufacturing, and validation processes. The project employs state-of-the-art proprietary Y-PREG technology, Automated Fiber Placement (AFP), and Continuous Fiber 3D Printing (FDM) to achieve scalable, high-precision fabrication. This approach allows for customizable RAM solutions that reduce radar cross-section (RCS) across a broad frequency range, as well as EMI shielding solutions. Advanced simulation models optimize the composites for specific configurations, reducing costly trials and enabling highly effective solutions.

Beyond technical advancements, ABBOT strengthens EU strategic autonomy by keeping critical technology development and production within EU. The consortium, comprising RTOs, SMEs, and industry leaders, fosters knowledge exchange and cross-border collaboration, driving EU leadership in defense innovation. With potential applications in dual-use sectors like aerospace and telecommunications, ABBOT supports EU growth beyond defense. Delivering proof-of-concept materials at TRL4, ABBOT positions EU as a leader in resilient, sustainable defense technologies, aligning with EDF, CDP and NATO’s priorities.

title: Harnessing Adaptive Renewables: Versatile Energy Supply Technology
objective:
The HARVEST (Harnessing Adaptive Renewables: Versatile Energy Supply Technology) project aims to revolutionize military power management by developing a lightweight, eco-designed wearable energy system. Current solutions burden soldiers with up to 12.5 kg of batteries for 72-hour missions, significantly impacting mobility and operational effectiveness. HARVEST addresses this challenge by integrating cutting-edge technologies to enhance soldier autonomy, reduce logistical burdens, and strengthen the European Defence Technological and Industrial Base (EDTIB).

Key innovations include:
•	Tellurium-free thermoelectric generators (TEGs), reducing reliance on scarce materials
•	Smart textile integration of power generation and distribution
•	Advanced solid-state lithium-sulphur batteries for improved safety and energy density
•	Versatile power generation combining TEG, triboelectric nanogenerators (TENG), and photovoltaic (PV) technologies
•	Integrated electromagnetic interference (EMI) shielding
•	Nanodrone power support for enhanced situational awareness

The project brings together a diverse consortium:
•	Danish Technological Institute (Denmark): Project coordinator, printed electronics expertise
•	TU Delft (Netherlands): Human-centric design and renewable energy integration
•	Fraunhofer IFAM (Germany): Advanced energy storage systems
•	NFM Technology (Norway): Military garment design and smart textiles
•	SilMach (France): Nanodrone technology

HARVEST aims to reduce power system weight by 5-29% while increasing operational duration by 24%. The project aligns with EU defence priorities, addressing the Capability Development Plan's focus on enhancing soldier systems, improving energy efficiency, and supporting strategic autonomy.
The 36-month project is structured into seven work packages, progressing from requirements gathering to technology development, system design, integration, and demonstration.

title: Innovative and Streamlined Water Prefiltration System for Military Personnel
objective: The INNOSWAMP project develops an innovative water pre-filtration system tailored for defence applications, addressing the limitations of current filtration technologies. By integrating graphene oxide (GO) enhanced materials with the design flexibility of additive manufacturing (AM), INNOSWAMP creates a modular, scalable, and high-performance system. This system effectively removes a wide range of contaminants, including heavy metals, salts, organic compounds, microorganisms, PFAS and even radioactive components, while minimizing energy consumption and waste. The project explores the use of polymers, ceramics, and metals, each optimized through AM and enhanced with specialized coatings, to create adaptable filters for diverse operational scenarios. Furthermore, INNOSWAMP fosters a resilient European supply chain and establishes a new baseline for common European standards for water quality in defence, promoting interoperability and EU technological sovereignty. This comprehensive approach enhances defence capabilities by providing a sustainable, readily deployable, and highly efficient water purification solution, crucial for ensuring operational readiness and minimizing logistical burdens in diverse military environments.

participants.name: Centre de transfert de technologies céramiques

title: METAsurfaces for the next generation of STEALTH platforms
objective:
Modern warfare requires being able to adjust in real-time to multiple evolving threats and be resilient in the face of networked defence systems. Today’s stealth solutions remain mostly passive and, thus, cannot provide adaptability, nor multifunctionality. METASTEALTH envisions to tackle this challenge by applying the recent concept of digital metasurfaces to stealth applications. Those active solutions are very promising as they can shape the scattered electromagnetic field at will in real-time, switching functionality on the fly, and adapting it to operational needs. 
METASTEALTH aims to achieve disruptive performance with 2 ground-breaking technologies: Self-Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces – SRIS (radar absorbers) and Time-Modulated Intelligent Surfaces – TMIS (radar deceptors), which provide multifunctionality as a key asset.
METASTEALTH will consider naval platforms to illustrate SRIS and TMIS paradigm-shifting potentialities. The project aims to demonstrate the suitability of those two concepts by designing, fabricating, and characterizing demonstrators to bring these technologies to TRL4. A flexible project methodology, meant to mitigate uncertainties due to the development of such cutting-edge solutions, will be used.
METASTEALTH, by bringing together 7 partners from 6 countries (France, Germany, Cyprus, Lithuania, Greece, and Spain), ambitions to take a leap forward to challenge the current stealth paradigm and deliver an unparalleled advantage to European naval, aerial, and terrestrial platforms. A technology roadmap for follow-up developments, with associated probabilities of success, will ensure the continuity of the vision towards higher TRLs and industrial scale-up.
METASTEALTH will provide scientific leadership to Europe in this new field, pave the way for the foundation of a European network to structure R&D efforts, and provide the European defence industry with a key technological advance.

title: Disruptive “NEURO-QUantum-AI technology symbiosis” for Real-Time Cognitive Monitoring and Decision Support to improve Pilot Control and Safety.
objective:
The project NEUROQUAD (Disruptive “NEURO-QUantum-AI technology symbiosis” for Real-Time Cognitive Monitoring and Decision Support to improve Pilot Control and Safety), specifically, aims a better deeper neuronal monitoring of military pilots. The reason for this selection is that currently there are relevant needs during military pilot tasks that until now have not been solved. The ability to monitor and predict not only pilot cognitive states but also pilot’s readiness and fitness for a mission in real-time is critical, yet current systems are inadequate for this purpose. Develop adaptive learning models that personalize cognitive state detection by integrating multimodal data (EEG, eye-tracking, heart rate variability) and continuously learning from the pilot's physiological data is a real need. Real-time pilot cognitive state monitoring and prediction would significantly enhance the integration between pilots and advanced highly automated military aircraft systems, opening the door to more adaptive and effective human-machine interactions. The goal is to revolutionize pilot performance by combining advanced neurotechnology, AI, and quantum computing to enhance cognitive abilities and decision-making in real-time.
Accurately monitoring a pilot's cognitive state in real-time using neurotechnology is difficult due to noise, individual variability, and the complex nature of brain signals. This makes it challenging to distinguish between different cognitive states (e.g., stress, fatigue, focus). Current systems and technologies fail to provide the necessary precision, personalization, feedback and immediacy needed to make informed decisions in high-stress environments and create adaptative interfaces with externals systems. 
NEUROQUAD’s Disruptive Solution proposes a system that could monitor and predict not only pilot cognitive states but also pilot’s readiness and fitness for a mission in real-time, providing a rapid assessment of their cognitive readiness.
keywords: Real-time and personalized pilot cognitive state monitoring and prediction; advanced neurotechnology and physiological monitoring; AI and quantum computing into a cohesive system;highly compact tech

participants.name: ZENTRUM FUR LUFT UND RAUMFAHRTMEDIZIN DER LUFTWAFFE

title: Quantum Agile and Resilient Military Communications
objective:
Q-ARM (Quantum Agile and Resilient Military Communications) aims to develop a crypto-agile, quantum-secure communication and decentralized identity management system, leveraging a hybrid combination of Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC) and Quantum Key Distribution (QKD). This system aims to provide secure, future-proof protection for authentication mechanisms, targeting defense and critical infrastructure environments.
The communication framework integrates multiple actors: a satellite, a backend server, and user devices, enabling secure exchanges at the message level. PQC algorithms will be used to encrypt and sign messages, for protection against both classical and quantum attacks. The project also leverages QKD to securely generate and distribute encryption keys, which will be combined through an XOR function with PQC signatures, adding another layer of security.
The system will incorporate blockchain technology to manage Decentralized Identities (DIDs). These DIDs will be verified using PQC signatures and QKD-enhanced authentication, ensuring secure access control and identity management. Blockchain will provide transparency and immutability for decentralized identity verification and secure transactions.
A key management system (KMS) will securely handle the QKD-generated keys required for signature encryption. Key synchronization between the server, satellite, and user devices ensures that the system operates efficiently and remains secure, even in dynamic environments.
The system offers a novel approach by combining PQC signatures with QKD-based keys, protecting authentication data throughout its transmission, from the user device to the backend server. The integration of blockchain-based decentralized identities, ensures an immutable, decentralized, no single point of failure identity management.

title: Silicon-based materials for the short-wave infrared range
objective: In the project Silfrared we propose to research a new technology for the fabrication of infrared (IR) photodetectors based on supersaturated silicon in combination with black silicon, for the near (NIR) and short wave (SWIR) infrared ranges, for low-level light or night vision applications. The unique combination of characteristics of this technology (CMOS compatibility, low cost fabrication, uncooled operation, high resolution and fast response time) provides strategic and competitive advantage with respect to existing technologies, providing the EU with improved capabilities in surveillance and reconnaissance operations. Knowledge on the properties and fabrication conditions of thick supersaturated silicon layers will be generated, including the analysis of microwave annealing as an innovative semiconductor processing technique, and the compatibility of these technologies with black silicon layers.

participants.name: FRICKE UND MALLAH MICROWAVE TECHNOLOGY GMBH

title: Advanced European RF Components for Wideband Array-Type Radar, Electronic Warfare and Communication Systems
objective: ARRAYS addresses the supply chain gaps in the availability of advanced Transmit / Receive Chain (TRC) components within the European market. While there have been considerable advancements in the overseas markets in the development of advanced wideband components, demonstrators and systems covering 18 GHz to 40 GHz and beyond, the European market in this area falls strongly behind. The supply chain issue imposes a limitation on European system houses within the defence industry that are forced to purchase and use overseas components subject to end-user restrictions such as International Traffic of Arms Regulation (ITAR) or Export Administration Regulations (EAR99). ARRAYS solution offers a holistic approach addressing the availability of advanced packaged RF components within the European market. The solution encompasses four major steps, paving the path to advanced all-European array systems, namely by covering the gaps of the European supply chain by critical GaN on SiC and SiGe component design covering the 18 GHz to 40 GHz frequency band, increasing the level of integration by combining the critical RF components into all-integrated TRM building blocks, employing novel advanced packaging solutions to address the mechanical and thermal stability, and furthermore using the Innovative “sandwich-type” on-board integration to comply with the constraints of a unit cell size of an antenna array operating up to 40 GHz.

participants.name: COMMISSARIAT A L ENERGIE ATOMIQUE ET AUX ENERGIES ALTERNATIVES

title: stuDy of Additive ManufActuring for low-cost, low-observable, hiGhly-deployable, expendablE/attritable tuRbojet engines​
objective: Currently, there is a growing trend in the development of both small-scale drone swarms and larger-scale UAV platforms, which can operate either autonomously or teamed with manned fighter aircraft, thus providing a mass advantage in conflict, which ensures air superiority thanks to increased combat capacity, lethality, and survivability. A mix of different size classes, from expendable air vehicles to higher-end, more sophisticated attritable/reusable autonomous systems is pursued. Key to reduce the cost and time to manufacture and deploy large numbers of such UAVs is the development of a low-cost yet high-performance, scalable propulsion system that can be manufactured rapidly in large quantities. Turbojet engines are the ideal candidates, due to their high specific thrust and ease of integration into multiple platforms. However, they feature a series of drawbacks that affect their potentialities: 1) they suffer from a relatively poor efficiency and therefore increased specific fuel consumption, which limits range and endurance; 2) they are still quite costly to be produced and require long lead times; 3) they feature a comparatively high IR signature, which limits survivability; 4) they feature relatively low fuel flexibility which significantly limits their deployability and flexibility. In DAMAGER, four enabling technologies will be investigated that can solve the above-mentioned challenges: 1) innovations in engine thermodynamic cycle to increase fuel efficiency; 2) advanced AM materials and processes to reduce manufacturing costs and times; 3) innovative nozzle design to minimize IR signature; 4) innovative combustion and injection concepts with high-efficient fuel flexibility. These technologies will be assessed using both numerical and experimental studies on turbojet components that will be tested in laboratory, with the aim of de-risking potential future exploitation and provide an invaluable database for validation of proposed concepts.

participants.name: INSTITUTUL NATIONAL DE CERCETARE-DEZVOLTARE TURBOMOTOARE - COMOTI

title: Innovative Thrust Chambers for Hydrogen Based Low Emissivity Propellants
objective:
The technology being advanced in this proposal is the design and development of an innovative
ceramic-based combustion chamber for aerospace engines (both aeronautics and space) powered by
hydrogen.
The primary goal is to leverage the high power and low visibility of H2/O2 and H2/air flames. These
unique characteristics, which offer significant advantages for defense systems (such as higher cruise
velocity and reduced detectability), are however accompanied by extremely high combustion
temperatures that exceed the capabilities of current combustion chambers, whose materials melt well
below the H2 flame temperature. To address this, an innovative combustion chamber made from ultrahigh
temperature ceramic matrix composites, inherently capable of wall thermal protection, will be
developed.
A secondary goal of this project is to foster European expertise in advanced defense technologies
related to high-temperature composite ceramic materials. This capability is essential for ensuring selfreliance
and advancing EU-based innovations in defense and aerospace applications. The project
includes initiatives like developing material processing technologies, setting up dedicated research
infrastructures, and establishing training programs to cultivate skilled talent. The integration of these
efforts could further reinforce the EU’s position as a leader in high-temperature composite ceramics and
support broader objectives of strategic autonomy in aerospace defense technologies.

participants.name: FRAUNHOFER GESELLSCHAFT ZUR FORDERUNG DER ANGEWANDTEN FORSCHUNG EV

title: Integrated Reconfigurable Operational Planning, Navigation, Communication, and Coordination
objective:
The IRONPLAN project aims to empower the EU with technological independence in developing current and next-generation Command and Control (C2) systems, ultimately creating the first 'smart,' transparent, and verifiable C2 system. This initiative will encompass the integration and extensive use of robotic and autonomous systems (RAS) across all areas of military operations. 
To achieve this goal, IRONPLAN will leverage groundbreaking technologies that have emerged from EU-funded civil projects, alongside developing any additional technologies that may be necessary. 
The effort to enhance the EU's technological sovereignty will be bolstered by establishing a comprehensive EU-wide supply chain and promoting new cross-border partnerships. The suite of technologies to be created for IRONPLAN will begin at Technology Readiness Level (TRL) 3, with the expectation of advancing to TRL 5 by the project's conclusion.

GIOUMPITEK MELETI SCHEDIASMOS YLOPOIISI KAI POLISI ERGON PLIROFORIKIS ETAIREIA PERIORISMENIS EFTHYNIS

title: Lunar and Cislunar Identification and Detection
objective:
The LUCID Project (Lunar and Cislunar Identification and Detection) is a European initiative led by SMEs to explore how to detect and respond to potential threats in the emerging cislunar space—the region between Earth and the Moon.
The project will combine ground-based sensors (radio and optical) with a simulated space-based patroller to track and identify spacecraft in lunar orbit. It will develop the first integrated communication system between ground and flight segments designed specifically for cislunar surveillance.
By simulating detection, risk assessment, and response scenarios, LUCID will demonstrate how Europe can autonomously survey and react to suspicious space activity. The project uses advanced technologies like AI and Dara Fusion and contributes to European space sovereignty, defence readiness, and strategic autonomy.
Funded by the European Defence Fund, LUCID supports cooperation with European international partners and prepares Europe to play a key role in the growing cislunar space economy and security landscape.

title: HYBRID AUTONOMOUS UNMANNED VEHICLES SWARM FOR SUBMARINE SPECIAL OPERATIONS
objective:
The comparative advantage of a submarine lies in its stealth and its ability to operate beneath the sea surface without being detected. However, submarine operations in enemy littoral waters are particularly challenging due to the significantly increased probability of detection. 
Submarine ISR operations, for example, in enemy coastal areas with a high concentration of enemy surface, underwater, and coastal sensors and units, drastically increase the likelihood of detection. For diesel submarines, this probability is even higher due to snorkeling, a procedure during which the submarine is highly exposed.
The PLEIADES Project aims to address this issue. It is based on the concept of deploying from a submarine a swarm of Hybrid Autonomous Unmanned Vehicles (HAUVs) to operate in enemy littoral waters. The HAUV swarm will be transported to the operational area by a wire guided or/and autonomous underwater carrier, similar to a torpedo, which will be launched from the submarine. This carrier will be capable of covering a distance of approximately 10 nautical miles. It will remain connected to the submarine staying  at a safe distance of more than 30 n.m. from the HAUV swarm's operational area. After arriving in the area of interest, the carrier will deploy the HAUV swarm to execute the planned tasks.

title: Photonic integrated coherent LIDAR with range and rate adaptability for early detection and precise locking on fast moving objects
objective: Coverage and situational awareness are maintained today by surveillance systems that use complementary sensing modalities including radars and cameras to detect and lock on objects classified as threats. Although effective in most cases, these systems have the basic constraint that their high-resolution aspect is based on cameras, which are vulnerable to poor lighting and adverse weather conditions, while the radars, which can maintain operation under these conditions, suffer from low imaging resolution. POINTER steps in with the ambition to develop a disruptive LIDAR technology that will combine the high-resolution aspect of cameras with the active and coherent nature of radars enabling high-resolution 4D imaging under any lighting or weather conditions. POINTER will use to this direction 3 main concepts. The first one is the use of the FMCW LIDAR technology for high detection sensitivity and direct velocity sensing of moving objects. The second one is the use of photonic integration for module miniaturization and scanning acceleration. The third one is an adaptive operation concept that can optimize the detection or the locking performance, depending on an AI-enabled analysis of 4D data in real time. Based on these concepts, POINTER will develop a portable LIDAR module with two operation modes and fast transitions (<1 ms) between them. The first mode will offer a range of 1000 m and a FOV of 30o×25o, and will be optimum for early detection, whereas the second one will offer a frame rate of 1000 FPS, an axial resolution of 3 cm and an angular resolution of 0.1o×0.1o, enabling a box of 2o×2o to remain precisely locked on a target that will be moving fast within the initial FOV. POINTER builds on previous efforts, aiming to transfer concepts from civilian to defense applications. The consortium consists of four SMEs and one RO, and has all the credentials to establish after reasonable steps the first supply chain for defense-grade coherent LIDAR products in Europe.

title: Quantum Computing with Finite Elements for crack propagation in defence applications
objective:
Cracks are one of the common defects caused by damages to the material. The reliability and safety of military operations heavily depend on the performance and survivability of vehicles and their armour. Being able to predict crack propagation with high accuracy is therefore critical, both from a protection and a (threat) ammunition point of view.
Methods for detection and evaluation of crack propagation, including those based on Finite Element (FE) simulation, still face a number of limitations: high computational costs, excessive computing time, lack of accuracy and precision. Quantum computing holds the promise to make these restrictions obsolete in the long run.
QANCOMFIN will design novel and disruptive sets of classical and quantum algorithms for integration into a commercially available FE Analysis (FEA) simulation package – the IMPETUS DEFENSE Solver – to design an entirely new version able to predict crack propagation and thereby reshape the defence industry and redefine market landscape. QANCOMFIN will leverage the unique feature of the IMPETUS DEFENSE Solver, the node splitting technique modelling cracks, and propose a novel approach drawing inspiration from the Extended FE Method (X-FEM) while introducing key improvements through reformulating the crack propagation scheme based on the energy principle. QANCOMFIN will evaluate and verify the novel algorithms by demonstrating higher prediction accuracy for two defence use cases.
To unlock the potential of quantum computing for FE crack propagation simulation in defence, QANCOMFIN brings together 3 high-profile SMEs and 2 leading research organisations from 4 countries. QANCOMFIN will be a starting point to make Europe’s defence ecosystem quantum-ready, leading to future strategic, technological and operational advantage: enhanced and accelerated materials design and performance, cost reduction and enhanced safety and operational reliability by minimising downtime and improving combat readiness.

title: Strategic Autonomous vehicles for BattlEfield Reconnaissance and logistics.
objective:
SABER sets forth a novel idea of an autonomous multi-agent system of collaborative flying and ground robots. This idea will transform aerial delivery and data gathering systems for combat zones on land, sea, and air. The disruptiveness lies in the idea of using existing drones and ground rovers as part of a universal multi-agent system which enables scalability and cost-effectiveness. The SABER project will research hardware and software components as add-on solutions to existing robotic platforms enhancing their autonomous capacities for near real-time situational awareness and deliveries as an easy-to-use service. 
SABER's primary research question is whether it is possible to use the same technologies for a variety of aerial delivery and data collection needs, and thereby enable economies of scale and widespread adoption across capabilities for last-mile aerial logistics and data collection for situational awareness (SA). For impact, avoiding vendor-specific solutions helps to strengthen the European defence supply chain and beyond. To that end, the research outcomes hold significant spin-off potential both within the defence sector and in civilian applications calling for aerial data and point-to-point aerial deliveries.

title: System for Enhanced Threat detection, Interception and soft-kill Neutralisation of Lethal drones
objective: The explosive growth of micro, mini and small-size Class I UAS has resulted in cheap and rapid acquisition of modified commercial off-the-shelf assets for use in the defence arena. Today’s battlefield is saturated with a variety of aerial threats, especially from UAS, as demonstrated in recent conflicts and wars. Numerous options exist to combat UAS swarms, but all possess notable shortcomings, especially with regards to detection accuracy and operational costs. SENTINEL-EMP proposes for the first time, the design of a breakthrough lower-layer anti-drone VSHORAD ground-to-air defence system that can be integrated with any medium calibre autocannon embodying an automatic shell feeder. The system utilises programmable, EMP-capable air bursting projectiles to disable such UAS threats (soft-kill), and incorporates advanced AI-based detection, tracking, and targeting capabilities by combining and analysing in real-time data from radar, optical sensors and RF direction finder for accurate trajectory calculation and firing. Each projectile contains miniaturised electronic circuits that allow it to detonate at close proximity to the UAS threat. A Universal Automatic Ammunition Programming System which can be integrated into any medium calibre autocannon, will program each shell just before firing, adjusting the timing or detonation pattern in real-time based on threat analysis provided by the AI algorithm. The EMP payload within the shell will create a high power EMP that disables UAS by frying their electronic or electrical circuits within a sphere of a few meters from the detonation. The SENTINEL-EMP system is highly scalable with a firing rate of at least 50 rounds per minute with the ability to store hundreds of ammunitions in the autocannon’s bank, overcoming the limitations of existing missile-based interceptor ground-to-air defence systems. It will be designed to operate automatically and autonomously 24/7 under the supervision of personnel at the Command & Control Centre.

title: SPACE DEBRIS REMOVAL SYSTEM USING CONCENTRATED SOLAR RAYS
objective:
SPADER will design a space debris removal system through R&T actions. The concept idea that has been described and published in several international aerospace papers, is to interact with the selected space object using concentrated solar rays, high power beam, in such a way that the object will either be evaporated or its original orbital elements be altered, and either fall into a diminishing energy orbit, and finally entering the atmosphere and burning out, or pushed to a higher energy orbit (re-orbited to a graveyard).

This system is composed of a unique double parabolic mirror system able to collect, concentrate and focus the solar light on the target at large distances, which can remove the space debris by total vaporization or local vaporization that will alter its orbital elements.

The main module (system of mirrors) will be designed as a non-foldable or foldable type, so that it can be launched with the currently used space mission carriers like any other satellite. In a follow-up development project , SPADER will be delivered as a full product ready for launch.

SPADER will pave the way for further spin-offs, it could be used to deflect asteroids having collision trajectories with Earth by vaporization of their material which will generate a reactive deflective force.
keywords: Space debris concentrated solar rays declutter geo-orbit zone interception vaporization burn-out atmosphere

title: Adaptive and Secure Technology-Enabling Reliable and Integrated Opto-acoustic underwater Networking
objective:
Efficient and secure underwater communication is vital for maritime uncrewed systems, addressing challenges posed by the underwater environment and facilitating classified information exchange through trusted digital gateways. The increasing underwater and seabed activities lead to a growing demand for smart adaptive communication systems that ensure security and flexibility. 
Building on the EDA-SALSA project, the overall objective of the ASTERION is to develop an open (i.e., non-proprietary) architecture for secure (TRANSEC, COMSEC/NETSEC) multi-modal self-adaptive underwater communication, with improved performance (higher rates, longer ranges, more robustness through adaptivity, directivity, etc.) and enabling interoperability between heterogeneous mobile and fixed underwater nodes.
With a consortium of 24 partners from 9 countries in the EU and Norway, ASTERION will empower Europe’s industry by researching cutting-edge solutions for secure adaptive digital underwater communications essential for military operations involving Maritime Unmanned Systems. The project has the potential to significantly boost growth for EU companies by leveraging advanced technologies and ensuring compliance with market requirements. 
Moreover, the flexible architecture and standardized protocols will enhance European defence capabilities, foster innovation among small companies and startups, and promote strategic independence from U.S. suppliers. By developing a standardized framework and encouraging cross-compatibility, ASTERION will reinforce Europe’s competitive edge, enabling industry leaders to capture market share and establish a strong position in the global technology landscape. Ultimately, ASTERION promises to drive growth through scalable, interoperable solutions that enhance innovation and cost efficiencies across defence and civilian markets, positioning European companies at the forefront of this high-potential sector.

title: A Human-Centred MSaaS Ecosystem for Enhanced Mission Planning and Execution via Battlefield Modelling, Adversarial AI, and Multi-domain Simulation Environments
objective:
BattleVerse envisions a cutting-edge military metaverse bridging real and virtual battle environments to support dynamic and effective mission planning and execution. Through multi-domain simulations, BattleVerse will provide military strategists and commanders with an interactive virtual battlespace, enriched by Digital Twins (DTs) of battle assets, landscapes, and weather. These DTs will allow for precise testing of multiple Courses of Action and simulate potential enemy responses, enhancing decision-making. Leveraging AI-driven scenario generation and reinforcement learning, BattleVerse will enable the virtual execution of complex mission scenarios, delivering actionable insights into battlefield dynamics and improving response times.
Aligned with standards like STANAG 4603 and HLA, BattleVerse’s service-based architecture follows the Modelling and Simulation as a Service paradigm, integrating cloud-native services for scalable deployment across edge and cloud infrastructures. Using physics-based simulations and generative AI, BattleVerse will offer physically-consistent simulations that evolve with real-time inputs from the Internet of Battlefield Things. This interoperable ecosystem will minimize the fragmentation that typically hinders large-scale military simulations, fostering collaborative, multi-domain planning.
BattleVerse aims to become a critical mission-readiness tool, adapting to defence needs through human-centred AI. With continuous feedback from European MoD experts and an agile development approach, BattleVerse will remain aligned with operational requirements. A proof-of-concept demonstrator will validate its efficacy in realistic scenarios, while an innovation hub will accelerate SME entry into defence simulation. Through these advancements, BattleVerse will support Europe’s defence autonomy and strengthen its strategic technological leadership across an increasingly complex, multi-domain operational landscape.

title: Boosting European Advanced Missile System Technologies (BEAST)
objective: BEAST (Boosting European Advanced Missile System Technology) aims to develop a modular future short-range missile (FSRM) for flexible multi-role use in air-to-air, ground based air defence, and air-to-ground. BEAST will thoroughly analyse and understand the requirements of future aerial operations while assessing available advanced technological options for FSRM including their expected capabilities. BEAST project main objective is to develop modular missile concepts including feasibility demonstration and de-risking of innovative key technologies and to derive a FSRM development roadmap. BEAST will set the strategic, operational, technological, and economic conditions for the joint development and procurement of a European modular FSRM able to opt-in future industrial partners and end-users from Europe.

title: Future Air System for European Tactical Transportation 2
objective:
Tactical transport aircrafts are the workhorses of battlefields, fulfilling missions like troop transport, logistics transportation, medical evacuation (MEDEVAC), and airdrop (cargo and troops alike) among other missions. The current picture of the worldwide mid-size tactical aircraft market clearly suggests there is a gap that could be filled with a new aircraft model, which has been called Future Midsize Tactical Cargo (FMTC).
Several of the most relevant aircraft makers in Europe have joint forces and knowledge in a Consortium named FASETT and its second phase named FASETT2 in order to jointly define the preliminary characteristics of FMTC, looking to combine some cutting-edge technologies with an affordable effort. This initial phase comprises technologies identification, feasibility study and risk assessment phase. 
This initial effort, under the support and funding of EU will establish the foundations of the Future Midsize Tactical Cargo aircraft, designed and manufactured in Europe and targeting to become the main midsize military transport aircraft of the western countries for the 21st century.

title: reconfiGurable Autonomous collaboRative UnmanneD Aircraft
objective:
As the geopolitical landscape and balance of power continue to shift, European military forces face a critical need for next-generation air combat systems capable of operating effectively as part of a Combat Cloud in highly contested theatres of operation. To date, current manned and unmanned aerial systems face significant limitations in autonomy, adaptability, and interoperability, especially in collaborative networked operations.
GARUDA project has the objective to establish in 36 months the foundational architecture for Unmanned Collaborative Combat Aircraft (U-CCA) systems capable of evolving with the dynamic and long-term operational needs of EU Defence forces. An innovative « Design and Simulate » process, involving incremental iterations between system design and operational simulation, is used to design U-CCA as a polymorphic platform, providing a fleet of design variants centred around a « common core ». This disruptive approach will guarantee the long-term adaptation and relevance of our U-CCA platform as it would facilitate the rapid development of new specialised variants as operational requirements and needs evolve (a substantial fraction of the U-CCA solution concepts would be shared by all configurations).
This ambitious global objective is decomposed into five specific objectives: (i) develop a common vision for U-CCA systems as part of a System of Systems, (ii) explore U-CCA technologies and design concepts that prioritise collaboration, variable autonomy levels, and modularity, (iii) enhance mission capabilities in contested environments, including manned-unmanned teaming, (iv) improve mission survivability and autonomy under challenging operational conditions (v) boost European technological independence and reduce reliance on non-EU suppliers.
Coordinated by ONERA (French RTO), GARUDA gathers 6 SMEs and 1 mid-cap, favouring a consortium of small innovative actors to take strong benefits from their agility and motivation.

title: Non Interferable Non Jammable Accurate Ammunition
objective:
NINJA² addresses the challenges faced in GNSS-contested and denied environments, where traditional precision-guided munitions fail due to jamming and spoofing. The project will develop an affordable, modular, accurate, and intelligent ammunition system to enhance precision and resilience in all modern weapon systems, including the possibility to upgrade existing systems.
The NINJA² philosophy is focussed around creating a Modular Open System Architecture (MOSA) which will be interoperable, flexible, and scalable for all smart ammunition systems. The core idea is to enable the development of munitions with shared components and standardized interfaces, allowing for rapid integration, upgrades, and customisation across different platforms and munition types.
The NINJA² system is centred around a guidance module for artillery ammunitions, incorporating advanced technologies for navigation and guidance. The system will also incorporate AI-based target detection, recognition, and tracking, as well as scene matching technology for terminal guidance.
The project, led by Rheinmetall, will involve collaboration between various European partners and leverage existing technologies to develop this scalable and modular open architecture for guided munitions. The ultimate goal is to provide a European system that reduces the need for large quantities of ammunition while increasing precision and minimizing wear and tear on weapon systems.
keywords: Micro-Electromechanical System based inertial navigation system

title: Prediction and Response of Effectors on Critical Infrastructure and Structural Environments
objective:
Current conflicts, such as those in Ukraine, the Middle East and the South China Sea, highlight the urgent requirement for advanced modelling tools to evaluate the effects of military operations on civilian infrastructure. To address these challenges, PRECISE offers three key features:
1.	High-resolution imagery collected from satellites and sensors (IR/EO, SAR and LIDAR) is annotated.
2.	AI-driven algorithms extract detailed information about structures, identifying vulnerabilities and patterns.
3.	PRECISE simulates various scenarios, including the impact of munitions on structures. This helps military planners to predict damage and make informed decisions to minimise collateral damage.

In line with the EDF-2024-LS-RA-CHALLENGE-SPACE-MSIAO, PRECISE incorporates four iterations to enable ongoing refinement. The project integrates outputs from the SPACE-MSIAO Challenge to improve data quality, modelling accuracy and predictive capabilities.
Furthermore, PRECISE contributes to the PESCO AMIDA-UT (Automated Modelling, Identification and Damage Assessment of Urban Terrain) initiative by providing tools for urban terrain analysis and damage prediction to enhance decision-making.

Thanks to nine partners and two affiliate entities from five EU member states: Belgium, Greece, France, Italy and Spain. The consortium is led by a mid-cap company (GMV) and comprises two large companies (INDRA and INDRA SPACE), two SMEs (COTESA and XENOMATIX), four cross-border SMEs (FLYSIGHT, IKH, EUROBIOS and MEWS FRANCE) and two research and technology organisations (RTOs) (RMA and BSC).

The project is organised into seven work packages (WPs), covering all the activities included in the call. PRECISE will be designed for both experts and non-expert users and will ensure its dual-use.

title: European Multifunction System Concept applied to Communications, Electronic Warfare and Radar
objective:
SCEPTER project seeks to combine radar, electronic warfare, and communication functions with adaptive cognitive features, enhancing situational awareness across air, land, and sea domains. It plans to create modular and interoperable systems, establishing standardized hardware and software to boost operational flexibility while minimizing electromagnetic spectrum consumption. The design will integrate advanced AESA antennas and Gallium Nitride technology to meet strict requirements for size, weight, and power in airborne platforms. Additionally, the program will tackle challenges in thermal management and signal integrity to ensure dependable performance across different environments.

The project will follow two parallel strategies: 
 - Developing essential components within Europe to secure technological autonomy and avoid dependency on foreign suppliers. This approach strengthens sovereignty by retaining control over critical subsystems for future modifications and upgrades. 
 - Adopting commercially available solutions where applicable to speed up development timelines and reduce costs. By combining internal innovation with off-the-shelf technologies, the project supports both self-sufficiency and efficiency in advancing Europe’s defense capabilities.

title: Second research action for Medical counter measures performed in the frame of the RESILIENCE FPA consortium
objective:
European Union faces security challenges due to international instability, exemplified by the war in Ukraine and the COVID-19 pandemic. Among these are rising Chemical, Biological, Radiological, and Nuclear (CBRN) threats. To enhance resilience, it is crucial to develop medical countermeasures (MCMs) to protect both military and civilian populations. To address this gap, the RESILIENCE FPA was established in 2022, providing a multi-annual action plan to strengthen European MCM research based on three pillars: i) Diagnosis, ii) Prevention & iii) Prophylaxis and Treatment.
The RESILIENCE-R-2024 project is the second research action under this plan. It is composed of 6 sub-projects: i) SP1-MANAGEMENT, ii) SP2-TOXINS: In vitro diagnostics of toxins intoxication and MCM against these toxins; iii) SP3-RAPID DIAG-Rapid on field diagnostic tests for B agents, iv) SP-4 BIOMARKERS B: New biomarkers of infection by B threat agents, v) SP-5 WOUNDS B: Treatment of wounds and injuries contaminated by B agents, and vi) SP-6-SCAV: Next generation of enzyme scavengers.
The action involves collaborations among 21 organizations from 11 European countries, including military, academic and industrial partners and aligns with the priorities of the European MoDs (Ministries of Defence). The ultimate goal is to develop new MCM against CB threats that fulfil military operational and functional requirements. The project will lead those innovative MCM candidates to TRL 4-5 toward knowledge generation, integration and studies activities, enhancing Europe's security, strategic EDTIB (European Defence Technology Industrial Base) autonomy and security of supply.
