title: European High-Performance Processor for RF Defense Applications
objective:
The deployment of RF-CMOS (Radio Frequency Complementary Metal-Oxide-Semiconductor) technologies for realizing RF Systems-On-a-Chip has revolutionized many applications through its unparalleled performance, manufacturability, and cost-efficiency.  However, many of the components used in defence applications originate from outside Europe today. This dependence on external component suppliers is not really acceptable for the defense industry, where components could be withheld or delayed for strategic reasons by foreign governments. It is therefore crucial for Europe to develop an intrinsically secure, ITAR free, supply chain for building RF-based RF defence systems which delivers state of the art components and is reliable, accessible and which can be preferentially offered to European OEMs for their future requirements.
The ePERFECT project aims to formulate a strategy for the development and ownership of an advanced RF-CMOS supply chain within Europe to address these issues. To achieve the overall project aims, European end users (OEMs) with different technology requirements and products will engage with European design groups and technology suppliers, to create components for the defence electronics industry over the coming decades.
The target of ePERFECT is to develop a fine pitch European RF-CMOS supply chain that can produce high-performance RF components like transceivers and processors. These components will be validated and incorporated into next-generation digital AESA Radar, wideband communication and narrowband communication systems. Furthermore, a first review of novel 3D RF systems packaging approaches for future development is being undertaken.
The capabilities of the RF-Processor and RF-Transceivers planned for this project will be exposed to the whole European OEM community to ensure maximum awareness of their capabilities through an OEM information exchange forum. Creating a critical mass in Europe around the core elements of the supply chain.

title: 5G NTN FOR EUROPEAN DEFENCE OPERATIONS
objective: 5G4Def delivers a standards-native 5G Non-Terrestrial Network capability tailored to defence “big-pipe” missions that demand very-high throughput, reliability, and security. Unlike direct-to-device initiatives, 5G4Def targets gigabit-class broadband per terminal using parabolic or flat- panel antennas, enabling resilient C2, ISR video, and multi-domain operations in contested electromagnetic environments. The solution is built on open interfaces and integrates a software-defined modem family that runs across heterogeneous hardware, from COTS gateways to flight-qualified payload compute. A hardened real- time platform, Osmium OS, provides secure/verified boot, signed updates, remote attestation, and deterministic performance.
keywords: Non- thematic calls for innovative and future-oriented defence solutions focused on SMEs

title: Advanced Breakthrough sYstems for SubSea Autonomy
objective: ABYSSA will foster the development of autonomous, resilient, and high-performance underwater systems capable of operating beyond 6000 meters. ABYSSA will overcome the most demanding challenges in energy, navigation, sensing, communication, and persistence. With underwater strategic infrastructure and resources such as submarine cables, energy pipelines, offshore energy platforms and deep-sea mines, increasingly targeted by both natural and intentional disruptions, ABYSSA aims to develop autonomous, resilient, and intelligent underwater systems capable of persistent surveillance, threat detection, and infrastructure protection in hostile maritime environments.
keywords: Generating knowledge, Integrating knowledge, Studies, Design

title: Augmented Capability for HIgh end soLdiErs 2
topic: Full-size demonstrators for next generation soldier systems
objective: ACHILE2 aims to develop a modular, open-architecture Next Generation Dismounted Soldier System tailored for modern high-intensity warfare. Building on the progress achieved through GOSSRA (PADR) and ACHILE (EDF 2021), ACHILE2 project aims to transform the Next Generation Dismounted Soldier System (NGDSS) into a fully modular, interoperable and open architecture, aligned with EU end-user needs and lessons from recent conflicts. The project scales from individual soldiers to platoons, incorporating advanced tech like AI-enabled threat detection, augmented reality, and wearable exosuits.

AEGIS-NET integrates vehicle-mounted nodes and fibre-optic tethered rocket-shaped UAV Interceptors, with human-on-the-loop control.

objective: AI-SHIELD proposes a private, deployable, sustainable and efficient large language model (LLM) becoming a strategic asset for the end-users. The project's solution enables enhanced intelligence analysis, situational awareness, mission planning, resource management and tactical decision support without compromising the data confidentiality and operational autonomy. The system builds upon open-source European LLMs from the OpenEuroLLM initiative and introduces a disruptive innovation through quantum-inspired model compression technology that reduces model size and energy consumption by up to 80% with minimal loss in performance.

objective: ASIMOV aims to establish the first European dual-use On-Orbit Servicing (OOS) System of Systems, enabling autonomous, secure, and sovereign operations across all orbital regimes (LEO, MEO, GEO). ASIMOV addresses Europe’s dependency on fragmented non-EU capabilities by integrating civil and defence developments into a unified architecture aligned with EDF goals for strategic autonomy, resilience, and deterrence in space. ASIMOV’s objective is to define, design, and validate a federated European OOS framework capable of refuelling, repairing, inspecting, and protecting satellites under contested or degraded conditions. The project will deliver detailed CONOPS and feasibility analyses for secure on-orbit operations, system and subsystem designs, and maturation of critical technologies up to TRL 6 in line with IHDCR.

type_of_action: Technological challenge research actions Privacy-preserving human-AI dialoguesystems –
objective: DIALOG-AI aims at setting up an ambitious multiyear evaluation challenge and live tests encouraging research and progress for the deployment of dialogue systems for defencepurposes. In the last few years, the rise of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI), Large Language Models (LLMs), chatbots and dialogue systems has marked a significant technological evolution. However, attempts at using these technologies in defencesystems have revealed a number of shortcomings, failing to achieve the required conditions for operational deployments. To tackle these problems and preserve European sovereignty in this domain, the DIALOG-AI project aims at setting up an ambitious multiyear evaluation challenge and live tests encouraging research and progress for the deployment of dialogue systems for defencepurposes, using innovative evaluation protocols specifically addressing defenceneeds.

objective: NEXTSWIR will demonstrate the benefits of HgCdTe avalanche photodiodes (APD) technology for long-range observation and early warning applications. The project aims to develop highly sensitive HgCdTe APD integrated in a large focal plane array and lasers for active and passive remote sensing in the eSWIR band. The image processing algorithm will be developed to enable high rate and short integration time and the project will produce a dedicated electronics testbench adaptable to the different use cases possible.

title: Disruptive Materials for Submarine Invisibility
type_of_action: organisations Non-thematic research actions by SMEs and research
objective: The PRIORITY project aims to develop advanced materials with extreme capabilities for a reduction of the acoustic and magnetic signature of submarines. The targeted materials have extraordinary capabilities that can significantly diminish the acoustic footprint of submarines to a mere one millionth of what is achievable with conventional materials. Additionally, these materials will impart antifouling and super hydrophobic properties, thereby mitigating drag forces. A remarkable 95% reduction in weight compared to traditional elastomeric materials, will enhance submarine maneuverability.
keywords: Non- thematic calls for innovative and future-oriented defence solutions focused on SMEs

title: Towards Robust 3D printed metallic components production for military applications by the development of innovative SURface FINishing and design protocols
type_of_action: organisations Non-thematic research actions by SMEs and research
objective: R3DSurfing aims at tackling the challenges faced by the Additive Manufacturing in post processing to make the technology suitable for deployment in military environments. The project will provide post-processing technologies for metal parts manufactured using the most common manufacturing technologies: Laser Powder Bed Fusion (PBF) and wire-Direct Energy deposition (DED). It introduces cutting-edge technologies, such as vibration-based support removal system, dry-suspension electrolyte-based electropolishing and surface protection (anodising) applied by immersion and gel-based surface finishing (anodising), for PBF made parts; and dry-suspension electrolyte electroblasting and surface finishing (passivation) applied by projection and gel-based passivation, for DED made parts.
keywords: Non- thematic calls for innovative and future-oriented defence solutions focused on SMEs

title: Second development SGA action for Medical Counter Measures against CBRN threats performed in the frame of the RESILIENCE FPA consortium
objective: RESILIENCE-D-2025 aims to develop two innovative medical countermeasures (MCMs) against nuclear and radiological (NR) threats, in response to urgent needs expressed by European Ministries of Defence. RESILIENCE-D-2025 aims to advancing two innovative MCMs against radiological and nuclear (RN) threats by addressing the treatment of Acute Radiation Syndromes frequent in RN exposure scenarios and Multi-Organ Failure (MOF), induced by combined injuries. The two disruptive Advanced Therapy Medicinal Products will be developed up to TRL 6.
keywords: Defence medical support, CBRN, biotech and human factors

title: Third research action for medical counter measures against CBRN threats performed in the frame of the RESILIENCE FPA consortium
objective: The project aims to develop innovative medical countermeasures to protect against chemical and biological threats, enhancing European security and reducing dependence on non-European sources. The project focuses on creating new diagnostic tools and treatments for chemical and biological threats, including bacterial and viral agents. It brings together a network of European partners from academia, industry, and the military to develop innovative solutions. The project aims to reduce Europe's reliance on non-European sources and enhance its autonomy in medical countermeasures. By developing new technologies and products, the project will contribute to European security and competitiveness and will also improve the protection of armed forces and civilians against chemical and biological threats.
keywords: Defence medical support, CBRN, biotech and human factors

title: REsilient Semiconductor Integrated design SysTem
objective: The RESIST project develops an integrated platform for IC design for military applications, combining advanced design tools, AI-driven automation, and continuous verification. The RESIST platform supports the full circuit design workflow, including military enhancements. RESIST addresses demanding military requirements and aims at improving the EU supply chain in respect of ICs for military uses. By reducing dependence on non-European suppliers and democratizing military chip design, RESIST strengthens Europe’s industrial competitiveness and strategic autonomy in defence semiconductor manufacturing.
keywords: Integrating knowledge, Studies, Design, System prototyping, Testing, Increasing efficiency

title: Robust privacy-preserving adaptive Human-AI dialoguE System for trustworthy and explaInable defence applicationS
type_of_action: Technological challenge research actions Privacy-preserving human-AI dialogue systems –Participation
objective: RHESIS aims to develop a transformative defence-oriented human-AI dialogue system able to interact with end-users in writing and by voice in all official EU languages. The RHESIS transformative defence-oriented human-AI dialogue system will have diverse defence applications, such as intelligence, strategic planning, tactical operations, and life-cycle support. RHESIS will perform defence domain adaptation of an existing EU Large Language Model (LLM), through advanced techniques for AI model customisation in order to immerse the model in the target domain’s language and knowledge patterns, and subsequent fine-tuning to specific defence dialogue scenarios.
keywords: Generating knowledge, Integrating knowledge, Studies, Design

title: High-temperature resistant and durable multispectral camouflage system
type_of_action: organisations Non-thematic research actions by SMEs and research
objective: ResistCAM aims to develop a new class of textiles that combine high-temperature resistant substrates with multifunctional nanofiber membranes and advanced coatings. The textile will be based on a hierarchically layered structure and will provide multispectral camouflage across the visible, infrared, and radar spectrum. The concept is base on an outer metallized or graphene-based surface with low emissivity, a porous nanofiber interlayer that insulates and equalizes heat flow, and inner supporting fabrics that provide mechanical robustness and user comfort.
keywords: Non- thematic calls for innovative and future-oriented defence solutions focused on SMEs

title: Strategic Autonomous Logistic Uncrewed Battlefield tRiage Intervention System
objective: SALUBRIS aims at bridging a critical gap in battlefield medical support by designing and validating an ecosystem of Robot Autonomous Systems (RAS) for casualty triage and evacuation (CASEVAC). The project focuses on multimodal CASEVAC, employing a mix of unmanned vehicles in different domains (land, sea, air) to perform casualty detection, recovery, and evacuation, through swarm coordination across the domains. Furthermore, the project employs sensors data fusion from available sources, including both wearable and nearable sensors, to provide autonomous triage based on the START methodology, employing explainable AI techniques to prioritize the casualties.
keywords: Defence medical support, CBRN, biotech and human factors

objective: SEQULITE is a European-designed, certified VPN that forms a “secure hose” between any two endpoints -the encrypted and authenticated tunnel never breaks as long as some connectivity exists. SEQULITE will develop a certified VPN [Virtual Private Network]. It does not matter if the link hops via LEO satellites, GEO relays, or public cellular backhauls –the encrypted and authenticated tunnel never breaks as long as some connectivity exists. SEQULITE boosts EU defenceautonomy by closing gaps in the Strategic Compass and EU defencepriorities in cyber and secure comm. It secures supply chains, reduces foreign tech reliance, and supports EDTIB through sovereign solutions and strategic partnerships.
keywords: Integrating knowledge, Studies, Design, System prototyping, Testing, Qualification, Certification

As a cornerstone for the next-generation military rotorcraft, the European Next Generation Helicopter Engine (ENGHE) will be essential to guaranteeing Europe’s operational autonomy. SHARP will identify dependences on external procurement, with the ambition to eliminate those entirely for a future engine.

It will also include heterogeneous swarms under a unified Ground Station, jam-resistant communications, high levels of autonomy, EW-contested and SLAM/GNSS-denied environments.

title: Soldier Operational Autonomous Resilient Energy System
type_of_action: Disruptive EDF research actions Non-thematic research actions targeting disruptive
objective: The project aims to develop a fully autonomous, water-propelled hydrogen ecosystem to provide energy and power for on-field operations. A portable solar-and wind-powered refilling unit will generate hydrogen and recharge cartridges directly in the field removing dependence on convoys or base resupply. A light wearable fuel cell system will enable soldiers to operate longer, safer, and with high-power devices such as advanced communications and unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs). A weight reduction of up to 70 % compared to current battery systems is foreseen.

title: Sonobuoy Optimization for Unmanned Deployment and Underwater Detection
objective: The SOUND2 concept is designed to provide an underwater surveillance solution based on sonobuoys deployment (i.e., a sonobuoysfield) via multi-domain Unmanned Vehicles. The project aims to develop an underwater surveillance solution supported by multi-domain Unmanned Vehicles to deploy & recover sonobuoys. The data gathered by the sonobuoyswill be processed using novel and trustworthy AI-enabled algorithms, to detect, localise, track, recogniseand classify underwater threats. Finally, the interface will allow the operators to access system information via a console and gain full situational awareness of the area.
keywords: Integrating knowledge, Studies, Design, System prototyping, Testing, Qualification

title: European Unified Aerial Security Ecosystem - Systemic Protection and Escalation Control for Threat Response in Europe
type_of_action: Disruptive EDF research actions Non-thematic research actions targeting disruptive
objective: SPECTRE delivers a disruptive, modular, and legally based System-of-Systems architecture to counter the rising threat of small Unmanned Aerial Systems (UAS). The project elevates the concept to the European level by combining technology, doctrine, and legal governance into a scalable and interoperable solution. SPECTRE develops a next-generation hybrid system that integrates advanced Unmanned Traffic Management technology and defencecounter-UAS information sharing into a self-learning and autonomous system enabling control over drone operations in multi-domain areas. Central to SPECTRE is its replacement of the static concept of operations with a reusable framework development document tailored for cross-sector deployment and EU/NATO mission integration.

title: Space-based Persistent ISR for Defence and Europe Reinforcement - phase 2
objective: SPIDER2 aims to develop a constellation of affordable satellites for space-based Intelligence, Surveillance, and Reconnaissance for defenceuse. SPIDER2 is the follow-up of the feasibility study SPIDER. It addresses developments of multi-mission affordable satellites constellations dedicated to space-based Intelligence/Surveillance/Reconnaissance (ISR) for defenceuse-cases compatible with national and EU initiatives. SPIDER2 will enable high reactivity, short revisit periods, and short end-to-end system latency, building on the foundations established by the initial SPIDER feasibility study.

title: Spin-based neural networks for RF processing and learning at the edge
type_of_action: Disruptive EDF research actions Non-thematic research actions targeting disruptive
objective: Spin Defender proposes a radically new way to process radio frequency (RF) signals. It substitutes digitization by spin-based learning, providing massive advantages for defenceoperations. The project aims to develop a digitisation free radio frequency (RF) spectrum analysis system, based on arrays of spintronics nanodevices incorporating in-hardware advanced AI processing (RF AI chip). The advantages for defenceoperations are a large instantaneous bandwidth, small size, high speed processing, low power consumption and adaptability to changing environments.

type_of_action: organisations Non-thematic research actions by SMEs and research
objective: TAC will develop a tactically responsive servicer capable of inspecting, capturing, and attaching to any spacecraft using a welding-based capture process. Unlike conventional docking systems requiring standardized interfaces and extreme precision, welding enables secure attachment through controlled contact, reducing complexity and cost while increasing mission flexibility and responsiveness. This enables new mission classes such as asset protection, relocation, deorbiting, and modular attachment thereby addressing Europe’s tactical space capability gaps.

title: Joint Air Missile Defence Initiative in Europa
objective:
This project, named Joint Air Missile Defence Initiative in Europe (JAMIE), aims to support the joint procurement of fully interoperable IRIS-T SLM systems, leveraging the collaborative framework provided by the European Defence Industrial Programme (EDIRPA). The initiative underscores the strategic importance of fostering cross-border defence cooperation within the European Union.
The successful operation of the IRIS-T SLM system in the Ukraine theatre of war has underlined its critical role and effectiveness in modern air defence and its critical relevance for European air defence in the medium range layer as a whole. 
Participating Member States (pMS) of this initiative at this time are Austria, Bulgaria, Estonia, Germany, Latvia and Slovenia with further states being in discussion of joining the cooperation (Lithuania, Denmark and further European countries have indicated joining JAMIE and their consideration of the IRIS-T SLM system for their national armed forces).
The total value of the common procurement in context of the action of the JAMIE programme is approximately 3.875.000.000 Euro.

title: Collaborative Procurement of Ammunition, 155 mm (Fast Track)
objective:
Small, medium and large calibers ammunition availability and stockpile are currently operational shortfalls in many Member States. This fact is severely impacting the military forces readiness and capacity to use their weapon systems ranging from small arms, anti-tank, mortar, tank as well as artillery. 

Consequently, many Member States identified ammunition as Most Promising & Most Needed capability. Accordingly, ammunition became a priority in short-term perspective for national procurement programs. In this regard, a stepwise approach is to facilitate collaborative procurement as a pragmatic solution for an efficient coordination among Member states for ammunition procurement within the EU (more efficiently and effectively together – pooling demands allows getting more competitive prices). In this light, the common procurement is avoiding the situation of Member States competing for securing ammunition stockpiles in one hand, and optimizing the use of the scarce financial resources (funds) available within the MS MoD budget on the other hand.

The overall objective of the European Defence Agency (EDA) collaborative project (Cat.B) Collaborative Procurement of Ammunition (CPoA) is to support the contributing Member States (MS) to procure ammunition of different types and calibers in a collaborative way (common procurement). Therefore, the CPoA aims to improve the security of the supply chains and optimize the use of EDTIB in the area of ammunition manufacturing. In accordance with the CPoA project legal framework (Project Arrangement/PA), EDA acts as the Contracting Authority in the name of the project contributing MS. Moreover, the CPoA PA mentions explicitly that the activities under the PA CPoA can be supported with relevant financial instruments of the European Union (EU), when in effect.

title: High Explosive 155mm Ammunition
objective:
The High Explosive 155mm Ammunition (HE 155mm) proposal addresses the common European procurement of 155mm high explosive (HE) artillery ammunition by four Member States (MS). 

The common procurement is led by Germany (DE), whose national procurement agency “Bundesamt für Ausrüstung, Informationstechnik und Nutzung der Bundeswehr (BAAINBw)”, acts as appointed procurement agent. 
The other participating MS (pMS) are Denmark (DK), the Netherlands (NL) and Estonia (EE).

title: EDIRPA Funding Proposal for Common Armoured Vehicle System (CAVS) Programme
objective:
Common Armoured Vehicle System (CAVS) Programme is a multinational program for development, procurement and life cycle management of modern armoured 6x6 vehicle system. The programme is led by Finland, who acts as a procurement agent and coordinator. The other Participant Member States are Latvia, Sweden and Germany.

The CAVS Programme is a comprehensive cooperation programme regarding the selected vehicle platform. The programme is based on similar capability needs between the Participant Member States, which generates foundation for further and in-depth cooperation and added value. This similarity is the backbone of the all contracts and decision-making in the CAVS Programme -cooperation. The CAVS Programme covers the whole life cycle of a military product including all necessary support systems and elements. 

Total value of the common procurements in the context of the actions in this proposal is 5 350 031 809,00 €. This value includes activities started after 24.02.2022 but not completed before the signature of the Grant Award and including all the activities planned in the future by the CAVS Programme Participant Member States. The CAVS Programme enables long term commitments as there are planned deliveries until 2037.  

The CAVS Programme consist of three levels covering the whole lifecycle of the vehicles: multinational cooperation under Technical Arrangement, industrial cooperation under joint frame agreements and national deliveries under national procurement contracts. The cooperation includes security of supply, and the agreements with the industry include requirements to include multinational cooperation also with local industry. The CAVS Programme has already now given new start to European 6x6 armoured wheeled vehicle serial manufacturing and it keeps ramping up the production capability in Europe and strengthening its resilience by including great number of SMEs into the supply chain. The Programme is open for new nations with similar needs.
